chapters.zh: 以色列王大卫儿子所罗门的箴言：
chapters.en: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;

chapters.zh: 要使人晓得智慧和训诲，分辨通达的言语，
chapters.en: To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;

chapters.zh: 使人处事领受智慧、仁义、公平、正直的训诲，
chapters.en: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;

chapters.zh: 使愚人灵明，使少年人有知识和谋略，
chapters.en: To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.

chapters.zh: 使智慧人听见，增长学问，使聪明人得著智谋
chapters.en: A wise will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:

chapters.zh: 使人明白箴言和譬喻，懂得智慧人的言词和谜语。
chapters.en: To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华是知识的开端；愚妄人藐视智慧和训诲。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD the beginning of knowledge: fools despise wisdom and instruction.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要听你父亲的训诲，不可离弃你母亲的法则（或译：指教）；
chapters.en: My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:

chapters.zh: 因为这要作你头上的华冠，你项上的金炼。
chapters.en: For they an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.

chapters.zh: 我儿，恶人若引诱你，你不可随从。
chapters.en: My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not.

chapters.zh: 他们若说：你与我们同去，我们要埋伏流人之血，要蹲伏害无罪之人；
chapters.en: If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:

chapters.zh: 我们好像阴间，把他们活活吞下；他们如同下坑的人，被我们囫囵吞了；
chapters.en: Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:

chapters.zh: 我们必得各样宝物，将所掳来的，装满房屋；
chapters.en: We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil:

chapters.zh: 你与我们大家同分，我们共用一个囊袋；
chapters.en: Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse:

chapters.zh: 我儿，不要与他们同行一道，禁止你脚走他们的路。
chapters.en: My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:

chapters.zh: 因为，他们的脚奔跑行恶；他们急速流人的血，
chapters.en: For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.

chapters.zh: 好像飞鸟，网罗设在眼前仍不躲避。
chapters.en: Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird.

chapters.zh: 这些人埋伏，是为自流己血；蹲伏，是为自害己命。
chapters.en: And they lay wait for their blood; they lurk privily for their lives.

chapters.zh: 凡贪恋财利的，所行之路都是如此；这贪恋之心乃夺去得财者之命。
chapters.en: So the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; taketh away the life of the owners thereof.

chapters.zh: 智慧在街市上呼喊，在宽阔处发声，
chapters.en: Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:

chapters.zh: 在热闹街头喊叫，在城门口，在城中发出言语，
chapters.en: She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, ,

chapters.zh: 说：你们愚昧人喜爱愚昧，亵慢人喜欢亵慢，愚顽人恨恶知识，要到几时呢？
chapters.en: How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?

chapters.zh: 你们当因我的责备回转；我要将我的灵浇灌你们，将我的话指示你们。
chapters.en: Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you.

chapters.zh: 我呼唤，你们不肯听从；我伸手，无人理会；
chapters.en: Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;

chapters.zh: 反轻弃我一切的劝戒，不肯受我的责备。
chapters.en: But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:

chapters.zh: 你们遭灾难，我就发笑；惊恐临到你们，我必嗤笑。
chapters.en: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;

chapters.zh: 惊恐临到你们，好像狂风；灾难来到，如同暴风；急难痛苦临到你们身上。
chapters.en: When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.

chapters.zh: 那时，你们必呼求我，我却不答应，恳切地寻找我，却寻不见。
chapters.en: Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:

chapters.zh: 因为，你们恨恶知识，不喜爱敬畏耶和华，
chapters.en: For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the LORD:

chapters.zh: 不听我的劝戒，藐视我一切的责备，
chapters.en: They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.

chapters.zh: 所以必吃自结的果子，充满自设的计谋 .。
chapters.en: Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人背道，必杀己身；愚顽人安逸，必害己命。
chapters.en: For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.

chapters.zh: 惟有听从我的，必安然居住，得享安静，不怕灾祸。
chapters.en: But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你若领受我的言语，存记我的命令，
chapters.en: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;

chapters.zh: 侧耳听智慧，专心求聪明，
chapters.en: So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, apply thine heart to understanding;

chapters.zh: 呼求明哲，扬声求聪明，
chapters.en: Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, liftest up thy voice for understanding;

chapters.zh: 寻找他，如寻找银子，搜求他，如搜求隐藏的珍宝，
chapters.en: If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as hid treasures;

chapters.zh: 你就明白敬畏耶和华，得以认识神。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God.

chapters.zh: 因为，耶和华赐人智慧；知识和聪明都由他口而出。
chapters.en: For the LORD giveth wisdom: out of his mouth knowledge and understanding.

chapters.zh: 他给正直人存留真智慧，给行为纯正的人作盾牌，
chapters.en: He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: a buckler to them that walk uprightly.

chapters.zh: 为要保守公平人的路，护庇虔敬人的道。
chapters.en: He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.

chapters.zh: 你也必明白仁义、公平、正直、一切的善道。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity; , every good path.

chapters.zh: 智慧必入你心；你的灵要以知识为美。
chapters.en: When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;

chapters.zh: 谋略必护卫你；聪明必保守你，
chapters.en: Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:

chapters.zh: 要救你脱离恶道（或译：恶人的道），脱离说乖谬话的人。
chapters.en: To deliver thee from the way of the evil , from the man that speaketh froward things;

chapters.zh: 那等人舍弃正直的路，行走黑暗的道，
chapters.en: Who leave the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;

chapters.zh: 欢喜作恶，喜爱恶人的乖僻，
chapters.en: Who rejoice to do evil, delight in the frowardness of the wicked;

chapters.zh: 在他们的道中弯曲，在他们的路上偏僻。
chapters.en: Whose ways crooked, and froward in their paths:

chapters.zh: 智慧要救你脱离淫妇，就是那油嘴滑舌的外女。
chapters.en: To deliver thee from the strange woman, from the stranger flattereth with her words;

chapters.zh: 他离弃幼年的配偶，忘了神的盟约。
chapters.en: Which forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of her God.

chapters.zh: 他的家陷入死地；他的路偏向阴间。
chapters.en: For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.

chapters.zh: 凡到他那里去的，不得转回，也得不著生命的路。
chapters.en: None that go unto her return again, neither take they hold of the paths of life.

chapters.zh: 智慧必使你行善人的道，守义人的路。
chapters.en: That thou mayest walk in the way of good , and keep the paths of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 正直人必在世上居住；完全人必在地上存留。
chapters.en: For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.

chapters.zh: 惟有恶人必然剪除；奸诈的，必然拔出。
chapters.en: But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.

chapters.zh: 我儿，不要忘记我的法则（或译：指教）；你心要谨守我的诫命；
chapters.en: My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:

chapters.zh: 因为他必将长久的日子，生命的年数与平安，加给你。
chapters.en: For length of days, and long life, and peace, shall they add to thee.

chapters.zh: 不可使慈爱、诚实离开你，要系在你颈项上，刻在你心版上。
chapters.en: Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:

chapters.zh: 这样，你必在神和世人眼前蒙恩宠，有聪明。
chapters.en: So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.

chapters.zh: 你要专心仰赖耶和华，不可倚靠自己的聪明，
chapters.en: Trust in the LORD with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.

chapters.zh: 在你一切所行的事上都要认定他，他必指引你的路。
chapters.en: In all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths.

chapters.zh: 不要自以为有智慧；要敬畏耶和华，远离恶事。
chapters.en: Be not wise in thine own eyes: fear the LORD, and depart from evil.

chapters.zh: 这便医治你的肚脐，滋润你的百骨。
chapters.en: It shall be health to thy navel, and marrow to thy bones.

chapters.zh: 你要以财物和一切初熟的土产尊荣耶和华。
chapters.en: Honour the LORD with thy substance, and with the firstfruits of all thine increase:

chapters.zh: 这样，你的仓房必充满有余；你的酒醡有新酒盈溢。
chapters.en: So shall thy barns be filled with plenty, and thy presses shall burst out with new wine.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你不可轻看耶和华的管教（或译：惩治），也不可厌烦他的责备；
chapters.en: My son, despise not the chastening of the LORD; neither be weary of his correction:

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华所爱的，他必责备，正如父亲责备所喜爱的儿子。
chapters.en: For whom the LORD loveth he correcteth; even as a father the son he delighteth.

chapters.zh: 得智慧，得聪明的，这人便为有福。
chapters.en: Happy the man findeth wisdom, and the man getteth understanding.

chapters.zh: 因为得智慧胜过得银子，其利益强如精金，
chapters.en: For the merchandise of it better than the merchandise of silver, and the gain thereof than fine gold.

chapters.zh: 比珍珠（或译：红宝石）宝贵；你一切所喜爱的，都不足与比较。
chapters.en: She more precious than rubies: and all the things thou canst desire are not to be compared unto her.

chapters.zh: 他右手有长寿，左手有富贵。
chapters.en: Length of days in her right hand; in her left hand riches and honour.

chapters.zh: 他的道是安乐；他的路全是平安。
chapters.en: Her ways ways of pleasantness, and all her paths peace.

chapters.zh: 他与持守他的作生命树；持定他的，俱各有福。
chapters.en: She a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her: and happy that retaineth her.

chapters.zh: 耶和华以智慧立地，以聪明定天，
chapters.en: The LORD by wisdom hath founded the earth; by understanding hath he established the heavens.

chapters.zh: 以知识使深渊裂开，使天空滴下甘露。
chapters.en: By his knowledge the depths are broken up, and the clouds drop down the dew.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要谨守真智慧和谋略，不可使他离开你的眼目。
chapters.en: My son, let not them depart from thine eyes: keep sound wisdom and discretion:

chapters.zh: 这样，他必作你的生命，颈项的美饰。
chapters.en: So shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace to thy neck.

chapters.zh: 你就坦然行路，不致碰脚。
chapters.en: Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.

chapters.zh: 你躺下，必不惧怕；你躺卧，睡得香甜。
chapters.en: When thou liest down, thou shalt not be afraid: yea, thou shalt lie down, and thy sleep shall be sweet.

chapters.zh: 忽然来的惊恐，不要害怕；恶人遭毁灭，也不要恐惧。
chapters.en: Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.

chapters.zh: 因为耶和华是你所倚靠的；他必保守你的脚不陷入网罗。
chapters.en: For the LORD shall be thy confidence, and shall keep thy foot from being taken.

chapters.zh: 你手若有行善的力量，不可推辞，就当向那应得的人施行。
chapters.en: Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do .

chapters.zh: 你那里若有现成的，不可对邻舍说：去罢，明天再来，我必给你。
chapters.en: Say not unto thy neighbour, Go, and come again, and to morrow I will give; when thou hast it by thee.

chapters.zh: 你的邻舍既在你附近安居，你不可设计害他。
chapters.en: Devise not evil against thy neighbour, seeing he dwelleth securely by thee.

chapters.zh: 人未曾加害与你，不可无故与他相争。
chapters.en: Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.

chapters.zh: 不可嫉妒强暴的人，也不可选择他所行的路。
chapters.en: Envy thou not the oppressor, and choose none of his ways.

chapters.zh: 因为，乖僻人为耶和华所憎恶；正直人为他所亲密。
chapters.en: For the froward abomination to the LORD: but his secret with the righteous.

chapters.zh: 耶和华咒诅恶人的家庭，赐福与义人的居所。
chapters.en: The curse of the LORD in the house of the wicked: but he blesseth the habitation of the just.

chapters.zh: 他讥诮那好讥诮的人，赐恩给谦卑的人。
chapters.en: Surely he scorneth the scorners: but he giveth grace unto the lowly.

chapters.zh: 智慧人必承受尊荣；愚昧人高升也成为羞辱。
chapters.en: The wise shall inherit glory: but shame shall be the promotion of fools.

chapters.zh: 众子啊，要听父亲的教训，留心得知聪明。
chapters.en: Hear, ye children, the instruction of a father, and attend to know understanding.

chapters.zh: 因我所给你们的是好教训；不可离弃我的法则（或译：指教）。
chapters.en: For I give you good doctrine, forsake ye not my law.

chapters.zh: 我在父亲面前为孝子，在母亲眼中为独一的娇儿。
chapters.en: For I was my father's son, tender and only in the sight of my mother.

chapters.zh: 父亲教训我说：你心要存记我的言语，遵守我的命令，便得存活。
chapters.en: He taught me also, and said unto me, Let thine heart retain my words: keep my commandments, and live.

chapters.zh: 要得智慧，要得聪明，不可忘记，也不可偏离我口中的言语。
chapters.en: Get wisdom, get understanding: forget not; neither decline from the words of my mouth.

chapters.zh: 不可离弃智慧，智慧就护卫你；要爱他，他就保守你。
chapters.en: Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.

chapters.zh: 智慧为首；所以，要得智慧。在你一切所得之内必得聪明 .（或译：用你一切所得的去换聪明）。
chapters.en: Wisdom the principal thing; get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.

chapters.zh: 高举智慧，他就使你高升；怀抱智慧，他就使你尊荣。
chapters.en: Exalt her, and she shall promote thee: she shall bring thee to honour, when thou dost embrace her.

chapters.zh: 他必将华冠加在你头上，把荣冕交给你。
chapters.en: She shall give to thine head an ornament of grace: a crown of glory shall she deliver to thee.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你要听受我的言语，就必延年益寿。
chapters.en: Hear, O my son, and receive my sayings; and the years of thy life shall be many.

chapters.zh: 我已指教你走智慧的道，引导你行正直的路。
chapters.en: I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.

chapters.zh: 你行走，脚步必不致狭窄；你奔跑，也不致跌倒。
chapters.en: When thou goest, thy steps shall not be straitened; and when thou runnest, thou shalt not stumble.

chapters.zh: 要持定训诲，不可放松；必当谨守，因为他是你的生命。
chapters.en: Take fast hold of instruction; let not go: keep her; for she thy life.

chapters.zh: 不可行恶人的路；不要走坏人的道。
chapters.en: Enter not into the path of the wicked, and go not in the way of evil .

chapters.zh: 要躲避，不可经过；要转身而去。
chapters.en: Avoid it, pass not by it, turn from it, and pass away.

chapters.zh: 这等人若不行恶，不得睡觉；不使人跌倒，睡卧不安；
chapters.en: For they sleep not, except they have done mischief; and their sleep is taken away, unless they cause to fall.

chapters.zh: 因为他们以奸恶吃饼，以强暴喝酒。
chapters.en: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.

chapters.zh: 但义人的路好像黎明的光，越照越明，直到日午。
chapters.en: But the path of the just as the shining light, that shineth more and more unto the perfect day.

chapters.zh: 恶人的道好像幽暗，自己不知因甚么跌倒。
chapters.en: The way of the wicked as darkness: they know not at what they stumble.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要留心听我的言词，侧耳听我的话语，
chapters.en: My son, attend to my words; incline thine ear unto my sayings.

chapters.zh: 都不可离你的眼目，要存记在你心中。
chapters.en: Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.

chapters.zh: 因为得著他的，就得了生命，又得了医全体的良药。
chapters.en: For they life unto those that find them, and health to all their flesh.

chapters.zh: 你要保守你心，胜过保守一切（或译：你要切切保守你心），因为一生的果效是由心发出。
chapters.en: Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it the issues of life.

chapters.zh: 你要除掉邪僻的口，弃绝乖谬的嘴。
chapters.en: Put away from thee a froward mouth, and perverse lips put far from thee.

chapters.zh: 你的眼目要向前正看；你的眼睛（原文是皮）当向前直观。
chapters.en: Let thine eyes look right on, and let thine eyelids look straight before thee.

chapters.zh: 要修平你脚下的路，坚定你一切的道。
chapters.en: Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.

chapters.zh: 不可偏向左右；要使你的脚离开邪恶。
chapters.en: Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: remove thy foot from evil.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要留心我智慧的话语，侧耳听我聪明的言词，
chapters.en: My son, attend unto my wisdom, bow thine ear to my understanding:

chapters.zh: 为要使你谨守谋略，嘴唇保存知识。
chapters.en: That thou mayest regard discretion, and thy lips may keep knowledge.

chapters.zh: 因为淫妇的嘴滴下蜂蜜；他的口比油更滑，
chapters.en: For the lips of a strange woman drop an honeycomb, and her mouth smoother than oil:

chapters.zh: 至终却苦似茵 𫈟，快如两刃的刀。
chapters.en: But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword.

chapters.zh: 他的脚下入死地；他脚步踏住阴间，
chapters.en: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.

chapters.zh: 以致他找不著生命平坦的道。他的路变迁不定，自己还不知道。
chapters.en: Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, thou canst not know .

chapters.zh: 众子啊，现在要听从我；不可离弃我口中的话。
chapters.en: Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.

chapters.zh: 你所行的道要离他远，不可就近他的房门，
chapters.en: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:

chapters.zh: 恐怕将你的尊荣给别人，将你的岁月给残忍的人；
chapters.en: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:

chapters.zh: 恐怕外人满得你的力量，你劳碌得来的，归入外人的家；
chapters.en: Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours in the house of a stranger;

chapters.zh: 终久，你皮肉和身体消毁，你就悲叹，
chapters.en: And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,

chapters.zh: 说：我怎么恨恶训诲，心中藐视责备，
chapters.en: And say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;

chapters.zh: 也不听从我师傅的话，又不侧耳听那教训我的人？
chapters.en: And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!

chapters.zh: 我在圣会里，几乎落在诸般恶中。
chapters.en: I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.

chapters.zh: 你要喝自己池中的水，饮自己井里的活水。
chapters.en: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.

chapters.zh: 你的泉源岂可涨溢在外？你的河水岂可流在街上？
chapters.en: Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad, rivers of waters in the streets.

chapters.zh: 惟独归你一人，不可与外人同用。
chapters.en: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers' with thee.

chapters.zh: 要使你的泉源蒙福；要喜悦你幼年所娶的妻。
chapters.en: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.

chapters.zh: 他如可爱的麀鹿，可喜的母鹿；愿他的胸怀使你时时知足，他的爱情使你常常恋慕。
chapters.en: the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你为何恋慕淫妇？为何抱外女的胸怀？
chapters.en: And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?

chapters.zh: 因为，人所行的道都在耶和华眼前；他也修平人一切的路。
chapters.en: For the ways of man before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.

chapters.zh: 恶人必被自己的罪孽捉住；他必被自己的罪恶如绳索缠绕。
chapters.en: His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.

chapters.zh: 他因不受训诲就必死亡；又因愚昧过甚，必走差了路。
chapters.en: He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你若为朋友作保，替外人击掌，
chapters.en: My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,

chapters.zh: 你就被口中的话语缠住，被嘴里的言语捉住。
chapters.en: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你既落在朋友手中，就当这样行才可救自己：你要自卑，去恳求你的朋友。
chapters.en: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend; go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.

chapters.zh: 不要容你的眼睛睡觉；不要容你的眼皮打盹。
chapters.en: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.

chapters.zh: 要救自己，如鹿脱离猎户的手，如鸟脱离捕鸟人的手。
chapters.en: Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand , and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人哪，你去察看蚂蚁的动作就可得智慧。
chapters.en: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:

chapters.zh: 蚂蚁没有元帅，没有官长，没有君王，
chapters.en: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,

chapters.zh: 尚且在夏天预备食物，在收割时聚敛粮食。
chapters.en: Provideth her meat in the summer, gathereth her food in the harvest.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人哪，你要睡到几时呢？你何时睡醒呢？
chapters.en: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?

chapters.zh: 再睡片时，打盹片时，抱著手躺卧片时，
chapters.en: a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

chapters.zh: 你的贫穷就必如强盗速来，你的缺乏彷佛拿兵器的人来到。
chapters.en: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.

chapters.zh: 无赖的恶徒，行动就用乖僻的口，
chapters.en: A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.

chapters.zh: 用眼传神，用脚示意，用指点划，
chapters.en: He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers;

chapters.zh: 心中乖僻，常设恶谋，布散纷争。
chapters.en: Frowardness in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.

chapters.zh: 所以，灾难必忽然临到他身；他必顷刻败坏，无法可治。
chapters.en: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.

chapters.zh: 耶和华所恨恶的有六样，连他心所憎恶的共有七样：
chapters.en: These six doth the LORD hate: yea, seven an abomination unto him:

chapters.zh: 就是高傲的眼，撒谎的舌，流无辜人血的手，
chapters.en: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,

chapters.zh: 图谋恶计的心，飞跑行恶的脚，
chapters.en: An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief,

chapters.zh: 吐谎言的假见证，并弟兄中布散纷争的人。
chapters.en: A false witness speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要谨守你父亲的诫命；不可离弃你母亲的法则（或译：指教），
chapters.en: My son, keep thy father's commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:

chapters.zh: 要常系在你心上，挂在你项上。
chapters.en: Bind them continually upon thine heart, tie them about thy neck.

chapters.zh: 你行走，他必引导你；你躺卧，他必保守你；你睡醒，他必与你谈论。
chapters.en: When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.

chapters.zh: 因为诫命是灯，法则（或译：指教）是光，训诲的责备是生命的道，
chapters.en: For the commandment a lamp; and the law light; and reproofs of instruction the way of life:

chapters.zh: 能保你远离恶妇，远离外女谄媚的舌头。
chapters.en: To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.

chapters.zh: 你心中不要恋慕他的美色，也不要被他眼皮勾引。
chapters.en: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.

chapters.zh: 因为，妓女能使人只剩一块饼；淫妇猎取人宝贵的生命。
chapters.en: For by means of a whorish woman to a piece of bread: and the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.

chapters.zh: 人若怀里搋火，衣服岂能不烧呢？
chapters.en: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned?

chapters.zh: 人若在火炭上走，脚岂能不烫呢？
chapters.en: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?

chapters.zh: 亲近邻舍之妻的，也是如此；凡挨近他的，不免受罚。
chapters.en: So he that goeth in to his neighbour's wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.

chapters.zh: 贼因饥饿偷窃充饥，人不藐视他，
chapters.en: do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry;

chapters.zh: 若被找著，他必赔还七倍，必将家中所有的尽都偿还。
chapters.en: But he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.

chapters.zh: 与妇人行淫的，便是无知；行这事的，必丧掉生命。
chapters.en: whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he doeth it destroyeth his own soul.

chapters.zh: 他必受伤损，必被凌辱；他的羞耻不得涂抹。
chapters.en: A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.

chapters.zh: 因为人的嫉恨成了烈怒，报仇的时候决不留情。
chapters.en: For jealousy the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.

chapters.zh: 甚么赎价，他都不顾；你虽送许多礼物，他也不肯干休。
chapters.en: He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你要遵守我的言语，将我的命令存记在心。
chapters.en: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.

chapters.zh: 遵守我的命令就得存活；保守我的法则（或译：指教），好像保守眼中的瞳人，
chapters.en: Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.

chapters.zh: 系在你指头上，刻在你心版上。
chapters.en: Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.

chapters.zh: 对智慧说：你是我的姊妹，称呼聪明为你的亲人，
chapters.en: Say unto wisdom, Thou my sister; and call understanding kinswoman:

chapters.zh: 他就保你远离淫妇，远离说谄媚话的外女。
chapters.en: That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger flattereth with her words.

chapters.zh: 我曾在我房屋的窗户内，从我窗櫺之间往外观看：
chapters.en: For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,

chapters.zh: 见愚蒙人内，少年人中，分明有一个无知的少年人，
chapters.en: And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,

chapters.zh: 从街上经过，走近淫妇的巷口，直往通他家的路去，
chapters.en: Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,

chapters.zh: 在黄昏，或晚上，或半夜，或黑暗之中。
chapters.en: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:

chapters.zh: 看哪，有一个妇人来迎接他，是妓女的打扮，有诡诈的心思。
chapters.en: And, behold, there met him a woman the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.

chapters.zh: 这妇人喧嚷，不守约束，在家里停不住脚，
chapters.en: (She loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house:

chapters.zh: 有时在街市上，有时在宽阔处，或在各巷口蹲伏，
chapters.en: Now without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)

chapters.zh: 拉住那少年人，与他亲嘴，脸无羞耻对他说：
chapters.en: So she caught him, and kissed him, with an impudent face said unto him,

chapters.zh: 平安祭在我这里，今日才还了我所许的愿。
chapters.en: peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.

chapters.zh: 因此，我出来迎接你，恳切求见你的面，恰巧遇见了你。
chapters.en: Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.

chapters.zh: 我已经用绣花毯子和埃及线织的花纹布铺了我的床。
chapters.en: I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved , with fine linen of Egypt.

chapters.zh: 我又用没药、沉香、桂皮薰了我的榻。
chapters.en: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.

chapters.zh: 你来，我们可以饱享爱情，直到早晨；我们可以彼此亲爱欢乐。
chapters.en: Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.

chapters.zh: 因为我丈夫不在家，出门行远路；
chapters.en: For the goodman not at home, he is gone a long journey:

chapters.zh: 他手拿银囊，必到月望才回家。
chapters.en: He hath taken a bag of money with him, will come home at the day appointed.

chapters.zh: 淫妇用许多巧言诱他随从，用谄媚的嘴逼他同行。
chapters.en: With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.

chapters.zh: 少年人立刻跟随他，好像牛往宰杀之地，又像愚昧人带锁炼去受刑罚，
chapters.en: He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;

chapters.zh: 直等箭穿他的肝，如同雀鸟急入网罗，却不知是自丧己命。
chapters.en: Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it for his life.

chapters.zh: 众子啊，现在要听从我，留心听我口中的话。
chapters.en: Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.

chapters.zh: 你的心不可偏向淫妇的道，不要入他的迷途。
chapters.en: Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.

chapters.zh: 因为，被他伤害仆倒的不少；被他杀戮的而且甚多。
chapters.en: For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong have been slain by her.

chapters.zh: 他的家是在阴间之路，下到死亡之宫。
chapters.en: Her house the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.

chapters.zh: 智慧岂不呼叫？聪明岂不发声？
chapters.en: Doth not wisdom cry? and understanding put forth her voice?

chapters.zh: 他在道旁高处的顶上，在十字路口站立，
chapters.en: She standeth in the top of high places, by the way in the places of the paths.

chapters.zh: 在城门旁，在城门口，在城门洞，大声说：
chapters.en: She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.

chapters.zh: 众人哪，我呼叫你们，我向世人发声。
chapters.en: Unto you, O men, I call; and my voice to the sons of man.

chapters.zh: 说：愚蒙人哪，你们要会悟灵明；愚昧人哪，你们当心里明白。
chapters.en: O ye simple, understand wisdom: and, ye fools, be ye of an understanding heart.

chapters.zh: 你们当听，因我要说极美的话；我张嘴要论正直的事。
chapters.en: Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips right things.

chapters.zh: 我的口要发出真理；我的嘴憎恶邪恶。
chapters.en: For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness an abomination to my lips.

chapters.zh: 我口中的言语都是公义，并无弯曲乖僻。
chapters.en: All the words of my mouth in righteousness; nothing froward or perverse in them.

chapters.zh: 有聪明的，以为明显，得知识的，以为正直。
chapters.en: They all plain to him that understandeth, and right to them that find knowledge.

chapters.zh: 你们当受我的教训，不受白银；宁得知识，胜过黄金。
chapters.en: Receive my instruction, and not silver; and knowledge rather than choice gold.

chapters.zh: 因为智慧比珍珠（或译：红宝石）更美；一切可喜爱的都不足与比较。
chapters.en: For wisdom better than rubies; and all the things that may be desired are not to be compared to it.

chapters.zh: 我 ─ 智慧以灵明为居所，又寻得知识和谋略。
chapters.en: I wisdom dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge of witty inventions.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华在乎恨恶邪恶；那骄傲、狂妄，并恶道，以及乖谬的口，都为我所恨恶。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD to hate evil: pride, and arrogancy, and the evil way, and the froward mouth, do I hate.

chapters.zh: 我有谋略和真知识；我乃聪明，我有能力。
chapters.en: Counsel mine, and sound wisdom: I understanding; I have strength.

chapters.zh: 帝王藉我坐国位；君王藉我定公平。
chapters.en: By me kings reign, and princes decree justice.

chapters.zh: 王子和首领，世上一切的审判官，都是藉我掌权。
chapters.en: By me princes rule, and nobles, all the judges of the earth.

chapters.zh: 爱我的，我也爱他；恳切寻求我的，必寻得见。
chapters.en: I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me.

chapters.zh: 丰富尊荣在我；恒久的财并公义也在我。
chapters.en: Riches and honour with me; , durable riches and righteousness.

chapters.zh: 我的果实胜过黄金，强如精金；我的出产超乎高银。
chapters.en: My fruit better than gold, yea, than fine gold; and my revenue than choice silver.

chapters.zh: 我在公义的道上走，在公平的路中行，
chapters.en: I lead in the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of judgment:

chapters.zh: 使爱我的，承受货财，并充满他们的府库。
chapters.en: That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance; and I will fill their treasures.

chapters.zh: 在耶和华造化的起头，在太初创造万物之先，就有了我。
chapters.en: The LORD possessed me in the beginning of his way, before his works of old.

chapters.zh: 从亘古，从太初，未有世界以前，我已被立。
chapters.en: I was set up from everlasting, from the beginning, or ever the earth was.

chapters.zh: 没有深渊，没有大水的泉源，我已生出。
chapters.en: When no depths, I was brought forth; when no fountains abounding with water.

chapters.zh: 大山未曾奠定，小山未有之先，我已生出。
chapters.en: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills was I brought forth:

chapters.zh: 耶和华还没有创造大地和田野，并世上的土质，我已生出。
chapters.en: While as yet he had not made the earth, nor the fields, nor the highest part of the dust of the world.

chapters.zh: 他立高天，我在那里；他在渊面的周围，划出圆圈。
chapters.en: When he prepared the heavens, I there: when he set a compass upon the face of the depth:

chapters.zh: 上使穹苍坚硬，下使渊源稳固，
chapters.en: When he established the clouds above: when he strengthened the fountains of the deep:

chapters.zh: 为沧海定出界限，使水不越过他的命令，立定大地的根基。
chapters.en: When he gave to the sea his decree, that the waters should not pass his commandment: when he appointed the foundations of the earth:

chapters.zh: 那时，我在他那里为工师，日日为他所喜爱，常常在他面前踊跃，
chapters.en: Then I was by him, one brought up : and I was daily delight, rejoicing always before him;

chapters.zh: 踊跃在他为人预备可住之地，也喜悦住在世人之间。
chapters.en: Rejoicing in the habitable part of his earth; and my delights with the sons of men.

chapters.zh: 众子啊，现在要听从我，因为谨守我道的，便为有福。
chapters.en: Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed keep my ways.

chapters.zh: 要听教训就得智慧，不可弃绝。
chapters.en: Hear instruction, and be wise, and refuse it not.

chapters.zh: 听从我、日日在我门口仰望、在我门框旁边等候的，那人便为有福。
chapters.en: Blessed the man that heareth me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.

chapters.zh: 因为寻得我的，就寻得生命，也必蒙耶和华的恩惠。
chapters.en: For whoso findeth me findeth life, and shall obtain favour of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 得罪我的，却害了自己的性命；恨恶我的，都喜爱死亡。
chapters.en: But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: all they that hate me love death.

chapters.zh: 智慧建造房屋，凿成七根柱子，
chapters.en: Wisdom hath builded her house, she hath hewn out her seven pillars:

chapters.zh: 宰杀牲畜，调和旨酒，设摆筵席；
chapters.en: She hath killed her beasts; she hath mingled her wine; she hath also furnished her table.

chapters.zh: 打发使女出去，自己在城中至高处呼叫，
chapters.en: She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,

chapters.zh: 说：谁是愚蒙人，可以转到这里来！又对那无知的人说：
chapters.en: Whoso simple, let him turn in hither: him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,

chapters.zh: 你们来，吃我的饼，喝我调和的酒。
chapters.en: Come, eat of my bread, and drink of the wine I have mingled.

chapters.zh: 你们愚蒙人，要舍弃愚蒙，就得存活，并要走光明的道。
chapters.en: Forsake the foolish, and live; and go in the way of understanding.

chapters.zh: 指斥亵慢人的，必受辱骂；责备恶人的，必被玷污。
chapters.en: He that reproveth a scorner getteth to himself shame: and he that rebuketh a wicked himself a blot.

chapters.zh: 不要责备亵慢人，恐怕他恨你；要责备智慧人，他必爱你。
chapters.en: Reprove not a scorner, lest he hate thee: rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.

chapters.zh: 教导智慧人，他就越发有智慧；指示义人，他就增长学问。
chapters.en: Give to a wise , and he will be yet wiser: teach a just , and he will increase in learning.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华是智慧的开端；认识至圣者便是聪明。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy understanding.

chapters.zh: 你藉著我，日子必增多，年岁也必加添。
chapters.en: For by me thy days shall be multiplied, and the years of thy life shall be increased.

chapters.zh: 你若有智慧，是与自己有益；你若亵慢，就必独自担当。
chapters.en: If thou be wise, thou shalt be wise for thyself: but thou scornest, thou alone shalt bear .

chapters.zh: 愚昧的妇人喧嚷；他是愚蒙，一无所知。
chapters.en: A foolish woman clamorous: simple, and knoweth nothing.

chapters.zh: 他坐在自己的家门口，坐在城中高处的座位上，
chapters.en: For she sitteth at the door of her house, on a seat in the high places of the city,

chapters.zh: 呼叫过路的，就是直行其道的人，
chapters.en: To call passengers who go right on their ways:

chapters.zh: 说：谁是愚蒙人，可以转到这里来！又对那无知的人说：
chapters.en: Whoso simple, let him turn in hither: and him that wanteth understanding, she saith to him,

chapters.zh: 偷来的水是甜的，暗吃的饼是好的。
chapters.en: Stolen waters are sweet, and bread in secret is pleasant.

chapters.zh: 人却不知有阴魂在他那里；他的客在阴间的深处。
chapters.en: But he knoweth not that the dead there; her guests in the depths of hell.

chapters.zh: 所罗门的箴言：智慧之子使父亲欢乐；愚昧之子叫母亲担忧。
chapters.en: The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son the heaviness of his mother.

chapters.zh: 不义之财毫无益处；惟有公义能救人脱离死亡。
chapters.en: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing: but righteousness delivereth from death.

chapters.zh: 耶和华不使义人受饥饿；恶人所欲的，他必推开。
chapters.en: The LORD will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish: but he casteth away the substance of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 手懒的，要受贫穷；手勤的，却要富足。
chapters.en: He becometh poor that dealeth a slack hand: but the hand of the diligent maketh rich.

chapters.zh: 夏天聚敛的，是智慧之子；收割时沉睡的，是贻羞之子。
chapters.en: He that gathereth in summer a wise son: he that sleepeth in harvest a son that causeth shame.

chapters.zh: 福祉临到义人的头；强暴蒙蔽恶人的口。
chapters.en: Blessings upon the head of the just: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 义人的纪念被称赞；恶人的名字必朽烂。
chapters.en: The memory of the just blessed: but the name of the wicked shall rot.

chapters.zh: 心中智慧的，必受命令；口里愚妄的，必致倾倒。
chapters.en: The wise in heart will receive commandments: but a prating fool shall fall.

chapters.zh: 行正直路的，步步安稳；走弯曲道的，必致败露。
chapters.en: He that walketh uprightly walketh surely: but he that perverteth his ways shall be known.

chapters.zh: 以眼传神的，使人忧患；口里愚妄的，必致倾倒。
chapters.en: He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow: but a prating fool shall fall.

chapters.zh: 义人的口是生命的泉源；强暴蒙蔽恶人的口。
chapters.en: The mouth of a righteous a well of life: but violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 恨能挑启争端；爱能遮掩一切过错。
chapters.en: Hatred stirreth up strifes: but love covereth all sins.

chapters.zh: 明哲人嘴里有智慧；无知人背上受刑杖。
chapters.en: In the lips of him that hath understanding wisdom is found: but a rod for the back of him that is void of understanding.

chapters.zh: 智慧人积存知识；愚妄人的口速致败坏。
chapters.en: Wise lay up knowledge: but the mouth of the foolish near destruction.

chapters.zh: 富户的财物是他的坚城；穷人的贫乏是他的败坏。
chapters.en: The rich man's wealth his strong city: the destruction of the poor their poverty.

chapters.zh: 义人的勤劳致生；恶人的进项致死（死：原文是罪）。
chapters.en: The labour of the righteous to life: the fruit of the wicked to sin.

chapters.zh: 谨守训诲的，乃在生命的道上；违弃责备的，便失迷了路。
chapters.en: He the way of life that keepeth instruction: but he that refuseth reproof erreth.

chapters.zh: 隐藏怨恨的，有说谎的嘴；口出谗谤的，是愚妄的人。
chapters.en: He that hideth hatred lying lips, and he that uttereth a slander, a fool.

chapters.zh: 多言多语难免有过；禁止嘴唇是有智慧。
chapters.en: In the multitude of words there wanteth not sin: but he that refraineth his lips wise.

chapters.zh: 义人的舌乃似高银；恶人的心所值无几。
chapters.en: The tongue of the just choice silver: the heart of the wicked little worth.

chapters.zh: 义人的口教养多人；愚昧人因无知而死亡。
chapters.en: The lips of the righteous feed many: but fools die for want of wisdom.

chapters.zh: 耶和华所赐的福使人富足，并不加上忧虑。
chapters.en: The blessing of the LORD, it maketh rich, and he addeth no sorrow with it.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人以行恶为戏耍；明哲人却以智慧为乐。
chapters.en: as sport to a fool to do mischief: but a man of understanding hath wisdom.

chapters.zh: 恶人所怕的，必临到他；义人所愿的，必蒙应允。
chapters.en: The fear of the wicked, it shall come upon him: but the desire of the righteous shall be granted.

chapters.zh: 暴风一过，恶人归于无有；义人的根基却是永久。
chapters.en: As the whirlwind passeth, so the wicked no : but the righteous an everlasting foundation.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人叫差他的人如醋倒牙，如烟薰目。
chapters.en: As vinegar to the teeth, and as smoke to the eyes, so the sluggard to them that send him.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华使人日子加多；但恶人的年岁必被减少。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD prolongeth days: but the years of the wicked shall be shortened.

chapters.zh: 义人的盼望必得喜乐；恶人的指望必致灭没。
chapters.en: The hope of the righteous gladness: but the expectation of the wicked shall perish.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的道是正直人的保障，却成了作孽人的败坏。
chapters.en: The way of the LORD strength to the upright: but destruction to the workers of iniquity.

chapters.zh: 义人永不挪移；恶人不得住在地上。
chapters.en: The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.

chapters.zh: 义人的口滋生智慧；乖谬的舌必被割断。
chapters.en: The mouth of the just bringeth forth wisdom: but the froward tongue shall be cut out.

chapters.zh: 义人的嘴能令人喜悦；恶人的口说乖谬的话。
chapters.en: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked frowardness.

chapters.zh: 诡诈的天平为耶和华所憎恶；公平的法码为他所喜悦。
chapters.en: A false balance abomination to the LORD: but a just weight his delight.

chapters.zh: 骄傲来，羞耻也来；谦逊人却有智慧。
chapters.en: pride cometh, then cometh shame: but with the lowly wisdom.

chapters.zh: 正直人的纯正必引导自己；奸诈人的乖僻必毁灭自己。
chapters.en: The integrity of the upright shall guide them: but the perverseness of transgressors shall destroy them.

chapters.zh: 发怒的日子资财无益；惟有公义能救人脱离死亡。
chapters.en: Riches profit not in the day of wrath: but righteousness delivereth from death.

chapters.zh: 完全人的义必指引他的路；但恶人必因自己的恶跌倒。
chapters.en: The righteousness of the perfect shall direct his way: but the wicked shall fall by his own wickedness.

chapters.zh: 正直人的义必拯救自己；奸诈人必陷在自己的罪孽中。
chapters.en: The righteousness of the upright shall deliver them: but transgressors shall be taken in naughtiness.

chapters.zh: 恶人一死，他的指望必灭绝；罪人的盼望也必灭没。
chapters.en: When a wicked man dieth, expectation shall perish: and the hope of unjust perisheth.

chapters.zh: 义人得脱离患难，有恶人来代替他。
chapters.en: The righteous is delivered out of trouble, and the wicked cometh in his stead.

chapters.zh: 不虔敬的人用口败坏邻舍；义人却因知识得救。
chapters.en: An hypocrite with mouth destroyeth his neighbour: but through knowledge shall the just be delivered.

chapters.zh: 义人享福，合城喜乐；恶人灭亡，人都欢呼。
chapters.en: When it goeth well with the righteous, the city rejoiceth: and when the wicked perish, shouting.

chapters.zh: 城因正直人祝福便高举，却因邪恶人的口就倾覆。
chapters.en: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted: but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.

chapters.zh: 藐视邻舍的，毫无智慧；明哲人却静默不言。
chapters.en: He that is void of wisdom despiseth his neighbour: but a man of understanding holdeth his peace.

chapters.zh: 往来传舌的，泄漏密事；心中诚实的，遮隐事情。
chapters.en: A talebearer revealeth secrets: but he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth the matter.

chapters.zh: 无智谋，民就败落；谋士多，人便安居。
chapters.en: Where no counsel , the people fall: but in the multitude of counsellors safety.

chapters.zh: 为外人作保的，必受亏损；恨恶击掌的，却得安稳。
chapters.en: He that is surety for a stranger shall smart : and he that hateth suretiship is sure.

chapters.zh: 恩德的妇女得尊荣；强暴的男子得资财。
chapters.en: A gracious woman retaineth honour: and strong retain riches.

chapters.zh: 仁慈的人善待自己；残忍的人扰害己身。
chapters.en: The merciful man doeth good to his own soul: but cruel troubleth his own flesh.

chapters.zh: 恶人经营，得虚浮的工价；撒义种的，得实在的果效。
chapters.en: The wicked worketh a deceitful work: but to him that soweth righteousness a sure reward.

chapters.zh: 恒心为义的，必得生命；追求邪恶的，必致死亡。
chapters.en: As righteousness to life: so he that pursueth evil to his own death.

chapters.zh: 心中乖僻的，为耶和华所憎恶；行事完全的，为他所喜悦。
chapters.en: They that are of a froward heart abomination to the LORD: but upright in way his delight.

chapters.zh: 恶人虽然连手，必不免受罚；义人的后裔必得拯救。
chapters.en: hand in hand, the wicked shall not be unpunished: but the seed of the righteous shall be delivered.

chapters.zh: 妇女美貌而无见识，如同金环带在猪鼻上。
chapters.en: a jewel of gold in a swine's snout, a fair woman which is without discretion.

chapters.zh: 义人的心愿尽得好处；恶人的指望致干忿怒。
chapters.en: The desire of the righteous only good: the expectation of the wicked wrath.

chapters.zh: 有施散的，却更增添；有吝惜过度的，反致穷乏。
chapters.en: There is that scattereth, and yet increaseth; and that withholdeth more than is meet, but to poverty.

chapters.zh: 好施舍的，必得丰裕；滋润人的，必得滋润。
chapters.en: The liberal soul shall be made fat: and he that watereth shall be watered also himself.

chapters.zh: 屯粮不卖的，民必咒诅他；情愿出卖的，人必为他祝福。
chapters.en: He that withholdeth corn, the people shall curse him: but blessing upon the head of him that selleth .

chapters.zh: 恳切求善的，就求得恩惠；惟独求恶的，恶必临到他身。
chapters.en: He that diligently seeketh good procureth favour: but he that seeketh mischief, it shall come unto him.

chapters.zh: 倚仗自己财物的，必跌倒；义人必发旺，如青叶。
chapters.en: He that trusteth in his riches shall fall: but the righteous shall flourish as a branch.

chapters.zh: 扰害己家的，必承受清风；愚昧人必作慧心人的仆人。
chapters.en: He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind: and the fool servant to the wise of heart.

chapters.zh: 义人所结的果子就是生命树；有智慧的，必能得人。
chapters.en: The fruit of the righteous a tree of life; and he that winneth souls wise.

chapters.zh: 看哪，义人在世尚且受报，何况恶人和罪人呢？
chapters.en: Behold, the righteous shall be recompensed in the earth: much more the wicked and the sinner.

chapters.zh: 喜爱管教的，就是喜爱知识；恨恶责备的，却是畜类。
chapters.en: Whoso loveth instruction loveth knowledge: but he that hateth reproof brutish.

chapters.zh: 善人必蒙耶和华的恩惠；设诡计的人，耶和华必定他的罪。
chapters.en: A good obtaineth favour of the LORD: but a man of wicked devices will he condemn.

chapters.zh: 人靠恶行不能坚立；义人的根必不动摇。
chapters.en: A man shall not be established by wickedness: but the root of the righteous shall not be moved.

chapters.zh: 才德的妇人是丈夫的冠冕；贻羞的妇人如同朽烂在他丈夫的骨中。
chapters.en: A virtuous woman a crown to her husband: but she that maketh ashamed as rottenness in his bones.

chapters.zh: 义人的思念是公平；恶人的计谋是诡诈。
chapters.en: The thoughts of the righteous right: the counsels of the wicked deceit.

chapters.zh: 恶人的言论是埋伏流人的血；正直人的口必拯救人。
chapters.en: The words of the wicked to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.

chapters.zh: 恶人倾覆，归于无有；义人的家必站得住。
chapters.en: The wicked are overthrown, and not: but the house of the righteous shall stand.

chapters.zh: 人必按自己的智慧被称赞；心中乖谬的，必被藐视。
chapters.en: A man shall be commended according to his wisdom: but he that is of a perverse heart shall be despised.

chapters.zh: 被人轻贱，却有仆人，强如自尊，缺少食物。
chapters.en: despised, and hath a servant, better than he that honoureth himself, and lacketh bread.

chapters.zh: 义人顾惜他牲畜的命；恶人的怜悯也是残忍。
chapters.en: A righteous regardeth the life of his beast: but the tender mercies of the wicked cruel.

chapters.zh: 耕种自己田地的，必得饱食；追随虚浮的，却是无知。
chapters.en: He that tilleth his land shall be satisfied with bread: but he that followeth vain void of understanding.

chapters.zh: 恶人想得坏人的网罗；义人的根得以结实。
chapters.en: The wicked desireth the net of evil : but the root of the righteous yieldeth .

chapters.zh: 恶人嘴中的过错是自己的网罗；但义人必脱离患难。
chapters.en: The wicked is snared by the transgression of lips: but the just shall come out of trouble.

chapters.zh: 人因口所结的果子，必饱得美福；人手所做的，必为自己的报应。
chapters.en: A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of mouth: and the recompence of a man's hands shall be rendered unto him.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人所行的，在自己眼中看为正直；惟智慧人肯听人的劝教。
chapters.en: The way of a fool right in his own eyes: but he that hearkeneth unto counsel wise.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人的恼怒立时显露；通达人能忍辱藏羞。
chapters.en: A fool's wrath is presently known: but a prudent covereth shame.

chapters.zh: 说出真话的，显明公义；作假见证的，显出诡诈。
chapters.en: speaketh truth sheweth forth righteousness: but a false witness deceit.

chapters.zh: 说话浮躁的，如刀刺人；智慧人的舌头却为医人的良药。
chapters.en: There is that speaketh like the piercings of a sword: but the tongue of the wise health.

chapters.zh: 口吐真言，永远坚立；舌说谎话，只存片时。
chapters.en: The lip of truth shall be established for ever: but a lying tongue but for a moment.

chapters.zh: 图谋恶事的，心存诡诈；劝人和睦的，便得喜乐。
chapters.en: Deceit in the heart of them that imagine evil: but to the counsellors of peace joy.

chapters.zh: 义人不遭灾害；恶人满受祸患。
chapters.en: There shall no evil happen to the just: but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.

chapters.zh: 说谎言的嘴为耶和华所憎恶；行事诚实的，为他所喜悦。
chapters.en: Lying lips abomination to the LORD: but they that deal truly his delight.

chapters.zh: 通达人隐藏知识；愚昧人的心彰显愚昧。
chapters.en: A prudent man concealeth knowledge: but the heart of fools proclaimeth foolishness.

chapters.zh: 殷勤人的手必掌权；懒惰的人必服苦。
chapters.en: The hand of the diligent shall bear rule: but the slothful shall be under tribute.

chapters.zh: 人心忧虑，屈而不伸；一句良言，使心欢乐。
chapters.en: Heaviness in the heart of man maketh it stoop: but a good word maketh it glad.

chapters.zh: 义人引导他的邻舍；恶人的道叫人失迷。
chapters.en: The righteous more excellent than his neighbour: but the way of the wicked seduceth them.

chapters.zh: 懒惰的人不烤打猎所得的；殷勤的人却得宝贵的财物。
chapters.en: The slothful roasteth not that which he took in hunting: but the substance of a diligent man precious.

chapters.zh: 在公义的道上有生命；其路之中并无死亡。
chapters.en: In the way of righteousness life; and the pathway no death.

chapters.zh: 智慧子听父亲的教训；亵慢人不听责备。
chapters.en: A wise son his father's instruction: but a scorner heareth not rebuke.

chapters.zh: 人因口所结的果子，必享美福；奸诈人必遭强暴。
chapters.en: A man shall eat good by the fruit of mouth: but the soul of the transgressors violence.

chapters.zh: 谨守口的，得保生命；大张嘴的，必致败亡。
chapters.en: He that keepeth his mouth keepeth his life: he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人羡慕，却无所得；殷勤人必得丰裕。
chapters.en: The soul of the sluggard desireth, and nothing: but the soul of the diligent shall be made fat.

chapters.zh: 义人恨恶谎言；恶人有臭名，且致惭愧。
chapters.en: A righteous hateth lying: but a wicked is loathsome, and cometh to shame.

chapters.zh: 行为正直的，有公义保守；犯罪的，被邪恶倾覆。
chapters.en: Righteousness keepeth upright in the way: but wickedness overthroweth the sinner.

chapters.zh: 假作富足的，却一无所有；装作穷乏的，却广有财物。
chapters.en: There is that maketh himself rich, yet nothing: that maketh himself poor, yet great riches.

chapters.zh: 人的资财是他生命的赎价；穷乏人却听不见威吓的话。
chapters.en: The ransom of a man's life his riches: but the poor heareth not rebuke.

chapters.zh: 义人的光明亮（原文是欢喜）；恶人的灯要熄灭 .。
chapters.en: The light of the righteous rejoiceth: but the lamp of the wicked shall be put out.

chapters.zh: 骄傲只启争竞；听劝言的，却有智慧。
chapters.en: Only by pride cometh contention: but with the well advised wisdom.

chapters.zh: 不劳而得之财必然消耗；勤劳积蓄的，必见加增。
chapters.en: Wealth by vanity shall be diminished: but he that gathereth by labour shall increase.

chapters.zh: 所盼望的迟延未得，令人心忧；所愿意的临到，却是生命树。
chapters.en: Hope deferred maketh the heart sick: but the desire cometh, a tree of life.

chapters.zh: 藐视训言的，自取灭亡；敬畏诫命的，必得善报。
chapters.en: Whoso despiseth the word shall be destroyed: but he that feareth the commandment shall be rewarded.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的法则（或译：指教）是生命的泉源，可以使人离开死亡的网罗。
chapters.en: The law of the wise a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.

chapters.zh: 美好的聪明使人蒙恩；奸诈人的道路崎岖难行。
chapters.en: Good understanding giveth favour: but the way of transgressors hard.

chapters.zh: 凡通达人都凭知识行事；愚昧人张扬自己的愚昧。
chapters.en: Every prudent dealeth with knowledge: but a fool layeth open folly.

chapters.zh: 奸恶的使者必陷在祸患里；忠信的使臣乃医人的良药。
chapters.en: A wicked messenger falleth into mischief: but a faithful ambassador health.

chapters.zh: 弃绝管教的，必致贫受辱；领受责备的，必得尊荣。
chapters.en: Poverty and shame him that refuseth instruction: but he that regardeth reproof shall be honoured.

chapters.zh: 所欲的成就，心觉甘甜；远离恶事，为愚昧人所憎恶。
chapters.en: The desire accomplished is sweet to the soul: but abomination to fools to depart from evil.

chapters.zh: 与智慧人同行的，必得智慧；和愚昧人作伴的，必受亏损。
chapters.en: He that walketh with wise shall be wise: but a companion of fools shall be destroyed.

chapters.zh: 祸患追赶罪人；义人必得善报。
chapters.en: Evil pursueth sinners: but to the righteous good shall be repayed.

chapters.zh: 善人给子孙遗留产业；罪人为义人积存资财。
chapters.en: A good leaveth an inheritance to his children's children: and the wealth of the sinner laid up for the just.

chapters.zh: 穷人耕种多得粮食，但因不义，有消灭的。
chapters.en: Much food the tillage of the poor: but there is destroyed for want of judgment.

chapters.zh: 不忍用杖打儿子的，是恨恶他；疼爱儿子的，随时管教。
chapters.en: He that spareth his rod hateth his son: but he that loveth him chasteneth him betimes.

chapters.zh: 义人吃得饱足；恶人肚腹缺粮。
chapters.en: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul: but the belly of the wicked shall want.

chapters.zh: 智慧妇人建立家室；愚妄妇人亲手拆毁。
chapters.en: Every wise woman buildeth her house: but the foolish plucketh it down with her hands.

chapters.zh: 行动正直的，敬畏耶和华；行事乖僻的，却藐视他。
chapters.en: He that walketh in his uprightness feareth the LORD: but perverse in his ways despiseth him.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人口中骄傲，如杖责打己身；智慧人的嘴必保守自己。
chapters.en: In the mouth of the foolish a rod of pride: but the lips of the wise shall preserve them.

chapters.zh: 家里无牛，槽头干净；土产加多乃凭牛力。
chapters.en: Where no oxen , the crib clean: but much increase by the strength of the ox.

chapters.zh: 诚实见证人不说谎话；假见证人吐出谎言。
chapters.en: A faithful witness will not lie: but a false witness will utter lies.

chapters.zh: 亵慢人寻智慧，却寻不著；聪明人易得知识。
chapters.en: A scorner seeketh wisdom, and not: but knowledge easy unto him that understandeth.

chapters.zh: 到愚昧人面前，不见他嘴中有知识。
chapters.en: Go from the presence of a foolish man, when thou perceivest not the lips of knowledge.

chapters.zh: 通达人的智慧在乎明白己道；愚昧人的愚妄乃是诡诈（或译：自叹）。
chapters.en: The wisdom of the prudent to understand his way: but the folly of fools deceit.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人犯罪，以为戏耍（或译：赎愆祭愚弄愚妄人）；正直人互相喜悦。
chapters.en: Fools make a mock at sin: but among the righteous favour.

chapters.zh: 心中的苦楚，自己知道；心里的喜乐，外人无干。
chapters.en: The heart knoweth his own bitterness; and a stranger doth not intermeddle with his joy.

chapters.zh: 奸恶人的房屋必倾倒；正直人的帐棚必兴盛。
chapters.en: The house of the wicked shall be overthrown: but the tabernacle of the upright shall flourish.

chapters.zh: 有一条路，人以为正，至终成为死亡之路。
chapters.en: There is a way which seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof the ways of death.

chapters.zh: 人在喜笑中，心也忧愁；快乐至极就生愁苦。
chapters.en: Even in laughter the heart is sorrowful; and the end of that mirth heaviness.

chapters.zh: 心中背道的，必满得自己的结果；善人必从自己的行为得以知足。
chapters.en: The backslider in heart shall be filled with his own ways: and a good man from himself.

chapters.zh: 愚蒙人是话都信；通达人步步谨慎。
chapters.en: The simple believeth every word: but the prudent looketh well to his going.

chapters.zh: 智慧人惧怕，就远离恶事；愚妄人却狂傲自恃。
chapters.en: A wise feareth, and departeth from evil: but the fool rageth, and is confident.

chapters.zh: 轻易发怒的，行事愚妄；设立诡计的，被人恨恶。
chapters.en: soon angry dealeth foolishly: and a man of wicked devices is hated.

chapters.zh: 愚蒙人得愚昧为产业；通达人得知识为冠冕。
chapters.en: The simple inherit folly: but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.

chapters.zh: 坏人俯伏在善人面前；恶人俯伏在义人门口。
chapters.en: The evil bow before the good; and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 贫穷人连邻舍也恨他；富足人朋友最多。
chapters.en: The poor is hated even of his own neighbour: but the rich many friends.

chapters.zh: 藐视邻舍的，这人有罪；怜悯贫穷的，这人有福。
chapters.en: He that despiseth his neighbour sinneth: but he that hath mercy on the poor, happy he.

chapters.zh: 谋恶的，岂非走入迷途么？谋善的，必得慈爱和诚实。
chapters.en: Do they not err that devise evil? but mercy and truth to them that devise good.

chapters.zh: 诸般勤劳都有益处；嘴上多言乃致穷乏。
chapters.en: In all labour there is profit: but the talk of the lips only to penury.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的财为自己的冠冕；愚妄人的愚昧终是愚昧。
chapters.en: The crown of the wise their riches: the foolishness of fools folly.

chapters.zh: 作真见证的，救人性命；吐出谎言的，施行诡诈。
chapters.en: A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful speaketh lies.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华的，大有倚靠；他的儿女也有避难所。
chapters.en: In the fear of the LORD strong confidence: and his children shall have a place of refuge.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华就是生命的泉源，可以使人离开死亡的网罗。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD a fountain of life, to depart from the snares of death.

chapters.zh: 帝王荣耀在乎民多；君王衰败在乎民少。
chapters.en: In the multitude of people the king's honour: but in the want of people the destruction of the prince.

chapters.zh: 不轻易发怒的，大有聪明；性情暴躁的，大显愚妄。
chapters.en: slow to wrath of great understanding: but hasty of spirit exalteth folly.

chapters.zh: 心中安静是肉体的生命；嫉妒是骨中的朽烂。
chapters.en: A sound heart the life of the flesh: but envy the rottenness of the bones.

chapters.zh: 欺压贫寒的，是辱没造他的主；怜悯穷乏的，乃是尊敬主。
chapters.en: He that oppresseth the poor reproacheth his Maker: but he that honoureth him hath mercy on the poor.

chapters.zh: 恶人在所行的恶上必被推倒；义人临死，有所投靠。
chapters.en: The wicked is driven away in his wickedness: but the righteous hath hope in his death.

chapters.zh: 智慧存在聪明人心中；愚昧人心里所存的，显而易见。
chapters.en: Wisdom resteth in the heart of him that hath understanding: but in the midst of fools is made known.

chapters.zh: 公义使邦国高举；罪恶是人民的羞辱。
chapters.en: Righteousness exalteth a nation: but sin a reproach to any people.

chapters.zh: 智慧的臣子蒙王恩惠；贻羞的仆人遭其震怒。
chapters.en: The king's favour toward a wise servant: but his wrath is him that causeth shame.

chapters.zh: 回答柔和，使怒消退；言语暴戾，触动怒气。
chapters.en: A soft answer turneth away wrath: but grievous words stir up anger.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的舌善发知识；愚昧人的口吐出愚昧。
chapters.en: The tongue of the wise useth knowledge aright: but the mouth of fools poureth out foolishness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的眼目无处不在；恶人善人，他都鉴察。
chapters.en: The eyes of the LORD in every place, beholding the evil and the good.

chapters.zh: 温良的舌是生命树；乖谬的嘴使人心碎。
chapters.en: A wholesome tongue a tree of life: but perverseness therein a breach in the spirit.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人藐视父亲的管教；领受责备的，得著见识。
chapters.en: A fool despiseth his father's instruction: but he that regardeth reproof is prudent.

chapters.zh: 义人家中多有财宝；恶人得利反受扰害。
chapters.en: In the house of the righteous much treasure: but in the revenues of the wicked is trouble.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的嘴播扬知识；愚昧人的心并不如此。
chapters.en: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge: but the heart of the foolish not so.

chapters.zh: 恶人献祭，为耶和华所憎恶；正直人祈祷，为他所喜悦。
chapters.en: The sacrifice of the wicked an abomination to the LORD: but the prayer of the upright his delight.

chapters.zh: 恶人的道路，为耶和华所憎恶；追求公义的，为他所喜爱。
chapters.en: The way of the wicked an abomination unto the LORD: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.

chapters.zh: 舍弃正路的，必受严刑；恨恶责备的，必致死亡。
chapters.en: Correction grievous unto him that forsaketh the way: he that hateth reproof shall die.

chapters.zh: 阴间和灭亡尚在耶和华眼前，何况世人的心呢？
chapters.en: Hell and destruction before the LORD: how much more then the hearts of the children of men?

chapters.zh: 亵慢人不爱受责备；他也不就近智慧人。
chapters.en: A scorner loveth not one that reproveth him: neither will he go unto the wise.

chapters.zh: 心中喜乐，面带笑容；心里忧愁，灵被损伤。
chapters.en: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance: but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken.

chapters.zh: 聪明人心求知识；愚昧人口吃愚昧。
chapters.en: The heart of him that hath understanding seeketh knowledge: but the mouth of fools feedeth on foolishness.

chapters.zh: 困苦人的日子都是愁苦；心中欢畅的，常享丰筵。
chapters.en: All the days of the afflicted evil: but he that is of a merry heart a continual feast.

chapters.zh: 少有财宝，敬畏耶和华，强如多有财宝，烦乱不安。
chapters.en: Better little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure and trouble therewith.

chapters.zh: 吃素菜，彼此相爱，强如吃肥牛，彼此相恨。
chapters.en: Better a dinner of herbs where love is, than a stalled ox and hatred therewith.

chapters.zh: 暴怒的人挑启争端；忍怒的人止息纷争。
chapters.en: A wrathful man stirreth up strife: but slow to anger appeaseth strife.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人的道像荆棘的篱笆；正直人的路是平坦的大道。
chapters.en: The way of the slothful as an hedge of thorns: but the way of the righteous made plain.

chapters.zh: 智慧子使父亲喜乐；愚昧人藐视母亲。
chapters.en: A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish man despiseth his mother.

chapters.zh: 无知的人以愚妄为乐；聪明的人按正直而行。
chapters.en: Folly joy to destitute of wisdom: but a man of understanding walketh uprightly.

chapters.zh: 不先商议，所谋无效；谋士众多，所谋乃成。
chapters.en: Without counsel purposes are disappointed: but in the multitude of counsellors they are established.

chapters.zh: 口善应对，自觉喜乐；话合其时，何等美好。
chapters.en: A man hath joy by the answer of his mouth: and a word in due season, how good !

chapters.zh: 智慧人从生命的道上升，使他远离在下的阴间。
chapters.en: The way of life above to the wise, that he may depart from hell beneath.

chapters.zh: 耶和华必拆毁骄傲人的家，却要立定寡妇的地界。
chapters.en: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud: but he will establish the border of the widow.

chapters.zh: 恶谋为耶和华所憎恶；良言乃为纯净。
chapters.en: The thoughts of the wicked an abomination to the LORD: but of the pure pleasant words.

chapters.zh: 贪恋财利的，扰害己家；恨恶贿赂的，必得存活。
chapters.en: He that is greedy of gain troubleth his own house; but he that hateth gifts shall live.

chapters.zh: 义人的心，思量如何回答；恶人的口吐出恶言。
chapters.en: The heart of the righteous studieth to answer: but the mouth of the wicked poureth out evil things.

chapters.zh: 耶和华远离恶人，却听义人的祷告。
chapters.en: The LORD far from the wicked: but he heareth the prayer of the righteous.

chapters.zh: 眼有光，使心喜乐；好信息，使骨滋润。
chapters.en: The light of the eyes rejoiceth the heart: a good report maketh the bones fat.

chapters.zh: 听从生命责备的，必常在智慧人中。
chapters.en: The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise.

chapters.zh: 弃绝管教的，轻看自己的生命；听从责备的，却得智慧。
chapters.en: He that refuseth instruction despiseth his own soul: but he that heareth reproof getteth understanding.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华是智慧的训诲；尊荣以前，必有谦卑。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD the instruction of wisdom; and before honour humility.

chapters.zh: 心中的谋算在乎人；舌头的应对由于耶和华。
chapters.en: The preparations of the heart in man, and the answer of the tongue, from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 人一切所行的，在自己眼中看为清洁；惟有耶和华衡量人心。
chapters.en: All the ways of a man clean in his own eyes; but the LORD weigheth the spirits.

chapters.zh: 你所做的，要交托耶和华，你所谋的，就必成立。
chapters.en: Commit thy works unto the LORD, and thy thoughts shall be established.

chapters.zh: 耶和华所造的，各适其用；就是恶人也为祸患的日子所造。
chapters.en: The LORD hath made all for himself: yea, even the wicked for the day of evil.

chapters.zh: 凡心里骄傲的，为耶和华所憎恶；虽然连手，他必不免受罚。
chapters.en: Every one proud in heart an abomination to the LORD: hand in hand, he shall not be unpunished.

chapters.zh: 因怜悯诚实，罪孽得赎；敬畏耶和华的，远离恶事。
chapters.en: By mercy and truth iniquity is purged: and by the fear of the LORD depart from evil.

chapters.zh: 人所行的，若蒙耶和华喜悦，耶和华也使他的仇敌与他和好。
chapters.en: When a man's ways please the LORD, he maketh even his enemies to be at peace with him.

chapters.zh: 多有财利，行事不义，不如少有财利，行事公义。
chapters.en: Better a little with righteousness than great revenues without right.

chapters.zh: 人心筹算自己的道路；惟耶和华指引他的脚步。
chapters.en: A man's heart deviseth his way: but the LORD directeth his steps.

chapters.zh: 王的嘴中有神语，审判之时，他的口必不差错。
chapters.en: A divine sentence in the lips of the king: his mouth transgresseth not in judgment.

chapters.zh: 公道的天平和秤都属耶和华；囊中一切法码都为他所定。
chapters.en: A just weight and balance the LORD'S: all the weights of the bag his work.

chapters.zh: 作恶，为王所憎恶，因国位是靠公义坚立。
chapters.en: an abomination to kings to commit wickedness: for the throne is established by righteousness.

chapters.zh: 公义的嘴为王所喜悦；说正直话的，为王所喜爱。
chapters.en: Righteous lips the delight of kings; and they love him that speaketh right.

chapters.zh: 王的震怒如杀人的使者；但智慧人能止息王怒。
chapters.en: The wrath of a king messengers of death: but a wise man will pacify it.

chapters.zh: 王的脸光使人有生命；王的恩典好像春云时雨。
chapters.en: In the light of the king's countenance life; and his favour as a cloud of the latter rain.

chapters.zh: 得智慧胜似得金子；选聪明强如选银子。
chapters.en: How much better to get wisdom than gold! and to get understanding rather to be chosen than silver!

chapters.zh: 正直人的道是远离恶事；谨守己路的，是保全性命。
chapters.en: The highway of the upright to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.

chapters.zh: 骄傲在败坏以先；狂心在跌倒之前。
chapters.en: Pride before destruction, and an haughty spirit before a fall.

chapters.zh: 心里谦卑与穷乏人来往，强如将掳物与骄傲人同分。
chapters.en: Better of an humble spirit with the lowly, than to divide the spoil with the proud.

chapters.zh: 谨守训言的，必得好处；倚靠耶和华的，便为有福。
chapters.en: He that handleth a matter wisely shall find good: and whoso trusteth in the LORD, happy he.

chapters.zh: 心中有智慧，必称为通达人；嘴中的甜言，加增人的学问。
chapters.en: The wise in heart shall be called prudent: and the sweetness of the lips increaseth learning.

chapters.zh: 人有智慧就有生命的泉源；愚昧人必被愚昧惩治。
chapters.en: Understanding a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools folly.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的心教训他的口，又使他的嘴增长学问。
chapters.en: The heart of the wise teacheth his mouth, and addeth learning to his lips.

chapters.zh: 良言如同蜂房，使心觉甘甜，使骨得医治。
chapters.en: Pleasant words an honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.

chapters.zh: 有一条路，人以为正，至终成为死亡之路。
chapters.en: There is a way that seemeth right unto a man, but the end thereof the ways of death.

chapters.zh: 劳力人的胃口使他劳力，因为他的口腹催逼他。
chapters.en: He that laboureth laboureth for himself; for his mouth craveth it of him.

chapters.zh: 匪徒图谋奸恶，嘴上彷佛有烧焦的火。
chapters.en: An ungodly man diggeth up evil: and in his lips as a burning fire.

chapters.zh: 乖僻人播散纷争；传舌的，离间密友。
chapters.en: A froward man soweth strife: and a whisperer separateth chief friends.

chapters.zh: 强暴人诱惑邻舍，领他走不善之道。
chapters.en: A violent man enticeth his neighbour, and leadeth him into the way not good.

chapters.zh: 眼目紧合的，图谋乖僻；嘴唇紧闭的，成就邪恶。
chapters.en: He shutteth his eyes to devise froward things: moving his lips he bringeth evil to pass.

chapters.zh: 白发是荣耀的冠冕，在公义的道上必能得著。
chapters.en: The hoary head a crown of glory, it be found in the way of righteousness.

chapters.zh: 不轻易发怒的，胜过勇士；治服己心的，强如取城。
chapters.en: slow to anger better than the mighty; and he that ruleth his spirit than he that taketh a city.

chapters.zh: 签放在怀里，定事由耶和华。
chapters.en: The lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 设筵满屋，大家相争，不如有块干饼，大家相安。
chapters.en: Better a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices strife.

chapters.zh: 仆人办事聪明，必管辖贻羞之子，又在众子中同分产业。
chapters.en: A wise servant shall have rule over a son that causeth shame, and shall have part of the inheritance among the brethren.

chapters.zh: 鼎为炼银，炉为炼金；惟有耶和华熬炼人心。
chapters.en: The fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold: but the LORD trieth the hearts.

chapters.zh: 行恶的，留心听奸诈之言；说谎的，侧耳听邪恶之语。
chapters.en: A wicked doer giveth heed to false lips; a liar giveth ear to a naughty tongue.

chapters.zh: 戏笑穷人的，是辱没造他的主；幸灾乐祸的，必不免受罚。
chapters.en: Whoso mocketh the poor reproacheth his Maker: he that is glad at calamities shall not be unpunished.

chapters.zh: 子孙为老人的冠冕；父亲是儿女的荣耀。
chapters.en: Children's children the crown of old men; and the glory of children their fathers.

chapters.zh: 愚顽人说美言本不相宜，何况君王说谎话呢？
chapters.en: Excellent speech becometh not a fool: much less do lying lips a prince.

chapters.zh: 贿赂在餽送的人眼中看为宝玉，随处运动都得顺利。
chapters.en: A gift a precious stone in the eyes of him that hath it: whithersoever it turneth, it prospereth.

chapters.zh: 遮掩人过的，寻求人爱；屡次挑错的，离间密友。
chapters.en: He that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth friends.

chapters.zh: 一句责备话深入聪明人的心，强如责打愚昧人一百下。
chapters.en: A reproof entereth more into a wise man than an hundred stripes into a fool.

chapters.zh: 恶人只寻背叛，所以必有严厉的使者奉差攻击他。
chapters.en: An evil seeketh only rebellion: therefore a cruel messenger shall be sent against him.

chapters.zh: 宁可遇见丢崽子的母熊，不可遇见正行愚妄的愚昧人。
chapters.en: Let a bear robbed of her whelps meet a man, rather than a fool in his folly.

chapters.zh: 以恶报善的，祸患必不离他的家。
chapters.en: Whoso rewardeth evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house.

chapters.zh: 纷争的起头如水放开，所以，在争闹之先必当止息争竞。
chapters.en: The beginning of strife when one letteth out water: therefore leave off contention, before it be meddled with.

chapters.zh: 定恶人为义的，定义人为恶的，这都为耶和华所憎恶。
chapters.en: He that justifieth the wicked, and he that condemneth the just, even they both abomination to the LORD.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人既无聪明，为何手拿价银买智慧呢？
chapters.en: Wherefore a price in the hand of a fool to get wisdom, seeing no heart ?

chapters.zh: 朋友乃时常亲爱，弟兄为患难而生。
chapters.en: A friend loveth at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.

chapters.zh: 在邻舍面前击掌作保乃是无知的人。
chapters.en: A man void of understanding striketh hands, becometh surety in the presence of his friend.

chapters.zh: 喜爱争竞的，是喜爱过犯；高立家门的，乃自取败坏。
chapters.en: He loveth transgression that loveth strife: he that exalteth his gate seeketh destruction.

chapters.zh: 心存邪僻的，寻不著好处；舌弄是非的，陷在祸患中。
chapters.en: He that hath a froward heart findeth no good: and he that hath a perverse tongue falleth into mischief.

chapters.zh: 生愚昧子的，必自愁苦；愚顽人的父毫无喜乐。
chapters.en: He that begetteth a fool to his sorrow: and the father of a fool hath no joy.

chapters.zh: 喜乐的心乃是良药；忧伤的灵使骨枯干。
chapters.en: A merry heart doeth good a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones.

chapters.zh: 恶人暗中受贿赂，为要颠倒判断。
chapters.en: A wicked taketh a gift out of the bosom to pervert the ways of judgment.

chapters.zh: 明哲人眼前有智慧；愚昧人眼望地极。
chapters.en: Wisdom before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool in the ends of the earth.

chapters.zh: 愚昧子使父亲愁烦，使母亲忧苦。
chapters.en: A foolish son a grief to his father, and bitterness to her that bare him.

chapters.zh: 刑罚义人为不善；责打君子为不义。
chapters.en: Also to punish the just not good, to strike princes for equity.

chapters.zh: 寡少言语的，有知识；性情温良的，有聪明。
chapters.en: He that hath knowledge spareth his words: a man of understanding is of an excellent spirit.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人若静默不言也可算为智慧；闭口不说也可算为聪明。
chapters.en: Even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise: he that shutteth his lips a man of understanding.

chapters.zh: 与众寡合的，独自寻求心愿，并恼恨一切真智慧。
chapters.en: Through desire a man, having separated himself, seeketh intermeddleth with all wisdom.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人不喜爱明哲，只喜爱显露心意。
chapters.en: A fool hath no delight in understanding, but that his heart may discover itself.

chapters.zh: 恶人来，藐视随来；羞耻到，辱骂同到。
chapters.en: When the wicked cometh, cometh also contempt, and with ignominy reproach.

chapters.zh: 人口中的言语如同深水；智慧的泉源好像涌流的河水。
chapters.en: The words of a man's mouth deep waters, the wellspring of wisdom a flowing brook.

chapters.zh: 瞻徇恶人的情面，偏断义人的案件，都为不善。
chapters.en: not good to accept the person of the wicked, to overthrow the righteous in judgment.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人张嘴启争端，开口招鞭打。
chapters.en: A fool's lips enter into contention, and his mouth calleth for strokes.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人的口自取败坏；他的嘴是他生命的网罗。
chapters.en: A fool's mouth his destruction, and his lips the snare of his soul.

chapters.zh: 传舌人的言语如同美食，深入人的心腹。
chapters.en: The words of a talebearer as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

chapters.zh: 做工懈怠的，与浪费人为弟兄。
chapters.en: He also that is slothful in his work is brother to him that is a great waster.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的名是坚固台；义人奔入便得安稳。
chapters.en: The name of the LORD a strong tower: the righteous runneth into it, and is safe.

chapters.zh: 富足人的财物是他的坚城，在他心想，犹如高墙。
chapters.en: The rich man's wealth his strong city, and as an high wall in his own conceit.

chapters.zh: 败坏之先，人心骄傲；尊荣以前，必有谦卑。
chapters.en: Before destruction the heart of man is haughty, and before honour humility.

chapters.zh: 未曾听完先回答的，便是他的愚昧和羞辱。
chapters.en: He that answereth a matter before he heareth , it folly and shame unto him.

chapters.zh: 人有疾病，心能忍耐；心灵忧伤，谁能承当呢？
chapters.en: The spirit of a man will sustain his infirmity; but a wounded spirit who can bear?

chapters.zh: 聪明人的心得知识；智慧人的耳求知识。
chapters.en: The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge.

chapters.zh: 人的礼物为他开路，引他到高位的人面前。
chapters.en: A man's gift maketh room for him, and bringeth him before great men.

chapters.zh: 先诉情由的，似乎有理；但邻舍来到，就察出实情。
chapters.en: first in his own cause just; but his neighbour cometh and searcheth him.

chapters.zh: 掣签能止息争竞，也能解散强胜的人。
chapters.en: The lot causeth contentions to cease, and parteth between the mighty.

chapters.zh: 弟兄结怨，劝他和好，比取坚固城还难；这样的争竞如同坚寨的门闩。
chapters.en: A brother offended than a strong city: and contentions like the bars of a castle.

chapters.zh: 人口中所结的果子，必充满肚腹；他嘴所出的，必使他饱足。
chapters.en: A man's belly shall be satisfied with the fruit of his mouth; with the increase of his lips shall he be filled.

chapters.zh: 生死在舌头的权下，喜爱他的，必吃他所结的果子。
chapters.en: Death and life in the power of the tongue: and they that love it shall eat the fruit thereof.

chapters.zh: 得著贤妻的，是得著好处，也是蒙了耶和华的恩惠。
chapters.en: findeth a wife findeth a good , and obtaineth favour of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 贫穷人说哀求的话；富足人用威吓的话回答。
chapters.en: The poor useth intreaties; but the rich answereth roughly.

chapters.zh: 滥交朋友的，自取败坏；但有一朋友比弟兄更亲密。
chapters.en: A man friends must shew himself friendly: and there is a friend sticketh closer than a brother.

chapters.zh: 行为纯正的贫穷人胜过乖谬愚妄的富足人。
chapters.en: Better the poor that walketh in his integrity, than perverse in his lips, and is a fool.

chapters.zh: 心无知识的，乃为不善；脚步急快的，难免犯罪。
chapters.en: Also, the soul without knowledge, not good; and he that hasteth with feet sinneth.

chapters.zh: 人的愚昧倾败他的道；他的心也抱怨耶和华。
chapters.en: The foolishness of man perverteth his way: and his heart fretteth against the LORD.

chapters.zh: 财物使朋友增多；但穷人朋友远离。
chapters.en: Wealth maketh many friends; but the poor is separated from his neighbour.

chapters.zh: 作假见证的，必不免受罚；吐出谎言的，终不能逃脱。
chapters.en: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and speaketh lies shall not escape.

chapters.zh: 好施散的，有多人求他的恩情；爱送礼的，人都为他的朋友。
chapters.en: Many will intreat the favour of the prince: and every man a friend to him that giveth gifts.

chapters.zh: 贫穷人，弟兄都恨他；何况他的朋友，更远离他！他用言语追随，他们却走了。
chapters.en: All the brethren of the poor do hate him: how much more do his friends go far from him? he pursueth words, they wanting .

chapters.zh: 得著智慧的，爱惜生命；保守聪明的，必得好处。
chapters.en: He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.

chapters.zh: 作假见证的，不免受罚；吐出谎言的，也必灭亡。
chapters.en: A false witness shall not be unpunished, and speaketh lies shall perish.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人宴乐度日是不合宜的；何况仆人管辖王子呢？
chapters.en: Delight is not seemly for a fool; much less for a servant to have rule over princes.

chapters.zh: 人有见识就不轻易发怒；宽恕人的过失便是自己的荣耀。
chapters.en: The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and his glory to pass over a transgression.

chapters.zh: 王的忿怒好像狮子吼叫；他的恩典却如草上的甘露。
chapters.en: The king's wrath as the roaring of a lion; but his favour as dew upon the grass.

chapters.zh: 愚昧的儿子是父亲的祸患；妻子的争吵如雨连连滴漏。
chapters.en: A foolish son the calamity of his father: and the contentions of a wife a continual dropping.

chapters.zh: 房屋钱财是祖宗所遗留的；惟有贤慧的妻是耶和华所赐的。
chapters.en: House and riches the inheritance of fathers: and a prudent wife from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 懒惰使人沉睡；懈怠的人必受饥饿。
chapters.en: Slothfulness casteth into a deep sleep; and an idle soul shall suffer hunger.

chapters.zh: 谨守诫命的，保全生命；轻忽己路的，必致死亡。
chapters.en: He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; he that despiseth his ways shall die.

chapters.zh: 怜悯贫穷的，就是借给耶和华；他的善行，耶和华必偿还。
chapters.en: He that hath pity upon the poor lendeth unto the LORD; and that which he hath given will he pay him again.

chapters.zh: 趁有指望，管教你的儿子；你的心不可任他死亡。
chapters.en: Chasten thy son while there is hope, and let not thy soul spare for his crying.

chapters.zh: 暴怒的人必受刑罚；你若救他，必须再救。
chapters.en: A man of great wrath shall suffer punishment: for if thou deliver , yet thou must do it again.

chapters.zh: 你要听劝教，受训诲，使你终久有智慧。
chapters.en: Hear counsel, and receive instruction, that thou mayest be wise in thy latter end.

chapters.zh: 人心多有计谋；惟有耶和华的筹算才能立定。
chapters.en: many devices in a man's heart; nevertheless the counsel of the LORD, that shall stand.

chapters.zh: 施行仁慈的，令人爱慕；穷人强如说谎言的。
chapters.en: The desire of a man his kindness: and a poor man better than a liar.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华的，得著生命；他必恒久知足，不遭祸患。
chapters.en: The fear of the LORD to life: and shall abide satisfied; he shall not be visited with evil.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人放手在盘子里，就是向口撤回，他也不肯。
chapters.en: A slothful hideth his hand in bosom, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.

chapters.zh: 鞭打亵慢人，愚蒙人必长见识；责备明哲人，他就明白知识。
chapters.en: Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, he will understand knowledge.

chapters.zh: 虐待父亲、撵出母亲的，是贻羞致辱之子。
chapters.en: He that wasteth father, chaseth away mother, a son that causeth shame, and bringeth reproach.

chapters.zh: 我儿，不可听了教训而又偏离知识的言语。
chapters.en: Cease, my son, to hear the instruction to err from the words of knowledge.

chapters.zh: 匪徒作见证戏笑公平；恶人的口吞下罪孽。
chapters.en: An ungodly witness scorneth judgment: and the mouth of the wicked devoureth iniquity.

chapters.zh: 刑罚是为亵慢人预备的；鞭打是为愚昧人的背预备的。
chapters.en: Judgments are prepared for scorners, and stripes for the back of fools.

chapters.zh: 酒能使人亵慢，浓酒使人喧嚷；凡因酒错误的，就无智慧。
chapters.en: Wine a mocker, strong drink raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise.

chapters.zh: 王的威吓如同狮子吼叫；惹动他怒的，是自害己命。
chapters.en: The fear of a king as the roaring of a lion: provoketh him to anger sinneth his own soul.

chapters.zh: 远离纷争是人的尊荣；愚妄人都爱争闹。
chapters.en: an honour for a man to cease from strife: but every fool will be meddling.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人因冬寒不肯耕种，到收割的时候，他必讨饭而无所得。
chapters.en: The sluggard will not plow by reason of the cold; shall he beg in harvest, and nothing.

chapters.zh: 人心怀藏谋略，好像深水，惟明哲人才能汲引出来。
chapters.en: Counsel in the heart of man deep water; but a man of understanding will draw it out.

chapters.zh: 人多述说自己的仁慈，但忠信人谁能遇著呢？
chapters.en: Most men will proclaim every one his own goodness: but a faithful man who can find?

chapters.zh: 行为纯正的义人，他的子孙是有福的！
chapters.en: The just walketh in his integrity: his children blessed after him.

chapters.zh: 王坐在审判的位上，以眼目驱散诸恶。
chapters.en: A king that sitteth in the throne of judgment scattereth away all evil with his eyes.

chapters.zh: 谁能说，我洁净了我的心，我脱净了我的罪？
chapters.en: Who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?

chapters.zh: 两样的法码，两样的升斗，都为耶和华所憎恶。
chapters.en: Divers weights, divers measures, both of them alike abomination to the LORD.

chapters.zh: 孩童的动作是清洁，是正直，都显明他的本性。
chapters.en: Even a child is known by his doings, whether his work pure, and whether right.

chapters.zh: 能听的耳，能看的眼，都是耶和华所造的。
chapters.en: The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the LORD hath made even both of them.

chapters.zh: 不要贪睡，免致贫穷；眼要睁开，你就吃饱。
chapters.en: Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty; open thine eyes, thou shalt be satisfied with bread.

chapters.zh: 买物的说：不好，不好；及至买去，他便自夸。
chapters.en: naught, naught, saith the buyer: but when he is gone his way, then he boasteth.

chapters.zh: 有金子和许多珍珠（或译：红宝石），惟有知识的嘴乃为贵重的珍宝。
chapters.en: There is gold, and a multitude of rubies: but the lips of knowledge a precious jewel.

chapters.zh: 谁为生人作保，就拿谁的衣服；谁为外人作保，谁就要承当。
chapters.en: Take his garment that is surety a stranger: and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

chapters.zh: 以虚谎而得的食物，人觉甘甜；但后来，他的口必充满尘沙。
chapters.en: Bread of deceit sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel.

chapters.zh: 计谋都凭筹算立定；打仗要凭智谋。
chapters.en: purpose is established by counsel: and with good advice make war.

chapters.zh: 往来传舌的，泄漏密事；大张嘴的，不可与他结交。
chapters.en: He that goeth about a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.

chapters.zh: 咒骂父母的，他的灯必灭，变为漆黑的黑暗。
chapters.en: Whoso curseth his father or his mother, his lamp shall be put out in obscure darkness.

chapters.zh: 起初速得的产业，终久却不为福。
chapters.en: An inheritance gotten hastily at the beginning; but the end thereof shall not be blessed.

chapters.zh: 你不要说，我要以恶报恶；要等候耶和华，他必拯救你。
chapters.en: Say not thou, I will recompense evil; wait on the LORD, and he shall save thee.

chapters.zh: 两样的法码为耶和华所憎恶；诡诈的天平也为不善。
chapters.en: Divers weights an abomination unto the LORD; and a false balance not good.

chapters.zh: 人的脚步为耶和华所定；人岂能明白自己的路呢？
chapters.en: Man's goings of the LORD; how can a man then understand his own way?

chapters.zh: 人冒失说，这是圣物，许愿之后才查问，就是自陷网罗。
chapters.en: a snare to the man devoureth holy, and after vows to make enquiry.

chapters.zh: 智慧的王簸散恶人，用碌碡滚轧他们。
chapters.en: A wise king scattereth the wicked, and bringeth the wheel over them.

chapters.zh: 人的灵是耶和华的灯，鉴察人的心腹。
chapters.en: The spirit of man the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts of the belly.

chapters.zh: 王因仁慈和诚实得以保全他的国位，也因仁慈立稳。
chapters.en: Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy.

chapters.zh: 强壮乃少年人的荣耀；白发为老年人的尊荣。
chapters.en: The glory of young men their strength: and the beauty of old men the gray head.

chapters.zh: 鞭伤除净人的罪恶；责打能入人的心腹。
chapters.en: The blueness of a wound cleanseth away evil: so stripes the inward parts of the belly.

chapters.zh: 王的心在耶和华手中，好像陇沟的水随意流转。
chapters.en: The king's heart in the hand of the LORD, the rivers of water: he turneth it whithersoever he will.

chapters.zh: 人所行的，在自己眼中都看为正；惟有耶和华衡量人心。
chapters.en: Every way of a man right in his own eyes: but the LORD pondereth the hearts.

chapters.zh: 行仁义公平比献祭更蒙耶和华悦纳。
chapters.en: To do justice and judgment more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.

chapters.zh: 恶人发达（发达：原文是灯），眼高心傲，这乃是罪。
chapters.en: An high look, and a proud heart, the plowing of the wicked, sin.

chapters.zh: 殷勤筹划的，足致丰裕；行事急躁的，都必缺乏。
chapters.en: The thoughts of the diligent only to plenteousness; but of every one hasty only to want.

chapters.zh: 用诡诈之舌求财的，就是自己取死；所得之财乃是吹来吹去的浮云。
chapters.en: The getting of treasures by a lying tongue a vanity tossed to and fro of them that seek death.

chapters.zh: 恶人的强暴必将自己扫除，因他们不肯按公平行事。
chapters.en: The robbery of the wicked shall destroy them; because they refuse to do judgment.

chapters.zh: 负罪之人的路甚是弯曲；至于清洁的人，他所行的乃是正直。
chapters.en: The way of man froward and strange: but the pure, his work right.

chapters.zh: 宁可住在房顶的角上，不在宽阔的房屋与争吵的妇人同住。
chapters.en: better to dwell in a corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman in a wide house.

chapters.zh: 恶人的心乐人受祸；他眼并不怜恤邻舍。
chapters.en: The soul of the wicked desireth evil: his neighbour findeth no favour in his eyes.

chapters.zh: 亵慢的人受刑罚，愚蒙的人就得智慧；智慧人受训诲，便得知识。
chapters.en: When the scorner is punished, the simple is made wise: and when the wise is instructed, he receiveth knowledge.

chapters.zh: 义人思想恶人的家，知道恶人倾倒，必致灭亡。
chapters.en: The righteous wisely considereth the house of the wicked: overthroweth the wicked for wickedness.

chapters.zh: 塞耳不听穷人哀求的，他将来呼吁也不蒙应允。
chapters.en: Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor, he also shall cry himself, but shall not be heard.

chapters.zh: 暗中送的礼物挽回怒气；怀中搋的贿赂止息暴怒。
chapters.en: A gift in secret pacifieth anger: and a reward in the bosom strong wrath.

chapters.zh: 秉公行义使义人喜乐，使作孽的人败坏。
chapters.en: joy to the just to do judgment: but destruction to the workers of iniquity.

chapters.zh: 迷离通达道路的，必住在阴魂的会中。
chapters.en: The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding shall remain in the congregation of the dead.

chapters.zh: 爱宴乐的，必致穷乏；好酒，爱膏油的，必不富足。
chapters.en: He that loveth pleasure a poor man: he that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.

chapters.zh: 恶人作了义人的赎价；奸诈人代替正直人。
chapters.en: The wicked a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.

chapters.zh: 宁可住在旷野，不与争吵使气的妇人同住。
chapters.en: better to dwell in the wilderness, than with a contentious and an angry woman.

chapters.zh: 智慧人家中积蓄宝物膏油；愚昧人随得来随吞下。
chapters.en: treasure to be desired and oil in the dwelling of the wise; but a foolish man spendeth it up.

chapters.zh: 追求公义仁慈的，就寻得生命、公义，和尊荣。
chapters.en: He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.

chapters.zh: 智慧人爬上勇士的城墙，倾覆他所倚靠的坚垒。
chapters.en: A wise scaleth the city of the mighty, and casteth down the strength of the confidence thereof.

chapters.zh: 谨守口与舌的，就保守自己免受灾难。
chapters.en: Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue keepeth his soul from troubles.

chapters.zh: 心骄气傲的人名叫亵慢；他行事狂妄，都出于骄傲。
chapters.en: Proud haughty scorner his name, who dealeth in proud wrath.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人的心愿将他杀害，因为他手不肯做工。
chapters.en: The desire of the slothful killeth him; for his hands refuse to labour.
