chapters.zh: 有终日贪得无餍的；义人施舍而不吝惜。
chapters.en: He coveteth greedily all the day long: but the righteous giveth and spareth not.

chapters.zh: 恶人的祭物是可憎的；何况他存恶意来献呢？
chapters.en: The sacrifice of the wicked abomination: how much more, he bringeth it with a wicked mind?

chapters.zh: 作假见证的必灭亡；惟有听真情而言的，其言长存。
chapters.en: A false witness shall perish: but the man that heareth speaketh constantly.

chapters.zh: 恶人脸无羞耻；正直人行事坚定。
chapters.en: A wicked man hardeneth his face: but the upright, he directeth his way.

chapters.zh: 没有人能以智慧、聪明、谋略敌挡耶和华。
chapters.en: no wisdom nor understanding nor counsel against the LORD.

chapters.zh: 马是为打仗之日预备的；得胜乃在乎耶和华。
chapters.en: The horse prepared against the day of battle: but safety of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 美名胜过大财；恩宠强如金银。
chapters.en: A name rather to be chosen than great riches, loving favour rather than silver and gold.

chapters.zh: 富户穷人在世相遇，都为耶和华所造。
chapters.en: The rich and poor meet together: the LORD the maker of them all.

chapters.zh: 通达人见祸藏躲；愚蒙人前往受害。
chapters.en: A prudent foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished.

chapters.zh: 敬畏耶和华心存谦卑，就得富有、尊荣、生命为赏赐。
chapters.en: By humility the fear of the LORD riches, and honour, and life.

chapters.zh: 乖僻人的路上有荆棘和网罗；保守自己生命的，必要远离。
chapters.en: Thorns snares in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them.

chapters.zh: 教养孩童，使他走当行的道，就是到老他也不偏离。
chapters.en: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.

chapters.zh: 富户管辖穷人；欠债的是债主的仆人。
chapters.en: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower servant to the lender.

chapters.zh: 撒罪孽的，必收灾祸；他逞怒的杖也必废掉。
chapters.en: He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.

chapters.zh: 眼目慈善的，就必蒙福，因他将食物分给穷人。
chapters.en: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor.

chapters.zh: 赶出亵慢人，争端就消除；纷争和羞辱也必止息。
chapters.en: Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.

chapters.zh: 喜爱清心的人因他嘴上的恩言，王必与他为友。
chapters.en: He that loveth pureness of heart, the grace of his lips the king his friend.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的眼目眷顾聪明人，却倾败奸诈人的言语。
chapters.en: The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, and he overthroweth the words of the transgressor.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人说：外头有狮子；我在街上就必被杀。
chapters.en: The slothful saith, a lion without, I shall be slain in the streets.

chapters.zh: 淫妇的口为深坑；耶和华所憎恶的，必陷在其中。
chapters.en: The mouth of strange women a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the LORD shall fall therein.

chapters.zh: 愚蒙迷住孩童的心，用管教的杖可以远远赶除。
chapters.en: Foolishness bound in the heart of a child; the rod of correction shall drive it far from him.

chapters.zh: 欺压贫穷为要利己的，并送礼与富户的，都必缺乏。
chapters.en: He that oppresseth the poor to increase his he that giveth to the rich, surely to want.

chapters.zh: 你须侧耳听受智慧人的言语，留心领会我的知识。
chapters.en: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.

chapters.zh: 你若心中存记，嘴上咬定，这便为美。
chapters.en: For a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.

chapters.zh: 我今日以此特特指教你，为要使你倚靠耶和华。
chapters.en: That thy trust may be in the LORD, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.

chapters.zh: 谋略和知识的美事，我岂没有写给你么？
chapters.en: Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,

chapters.zh: 要使你知道真言的实理，你好将真言回覆那打发你来的人。
chapters.en: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?

chapters.zh: 贫穷人，你不可因他贫穷就抢夺他的物，也不可在城门口欺压困苦人；
chapters.en: Rob not the poor, because he poor: neither oppress the afflicted in the gate:

chapters.zh: 因耶和华必为他辨屈；抢夺他的，耶和华必夺取那人的命。
chapters.en: For the LORD will plead their cause, and spoil the soul of those that spoiled them.

chapters.zh: 好生气的人，不可与他结交；暴怒的人，不可与他来往；
chapters.en: Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go:

chapters.zh: 恐怕你效法他的行为，自己就陷在网罗里。
chapters.en: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.

chapters.zh: 不要与人击掌，不要为欠债的作保。
chapters.en: Be not thou of them that strike hands, of them that are sureties for debts.

chapters.zh: 你若没有甚么偿还，何必使人夺去你睡卧的床呢？
chapters.en: If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?

chapters.zh: 你先祖所立的地界，你不可挪移。
chapters.en: Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.

chapters.zh: 你看见办事殷勤的人么？他必站在君王面前，必不站在下贱人面前。
chapters.en: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean .

chapters.zh: 你若与官长坐席，要留意在你面前的是谁。
chapters.en: When thou sittest to eat with a ruler, consider diligently what before thee:

chapters.zh: 你若是贪食的，就当拿刀放在喉咙上。
chapters.en: And put a knife to thy throat, if thou a man given to appetite.

chapters.zh: 不可贪恋他的美食，因为是哄人的食物。
chapters.en: Be not desirous of his dainties: for they deceitful meat.

chapters.zh: 不要劳碌求富，休仗自己的聪明。
chapters.en: Labour not to be rich: cease from thine own wisdom.

chapters.zh: 你岂要定睛在虚无的钱财上么？因钱财必长翅膀，如鹰向天飞去。
chapters.en: Wilt thou set thine eyes upon that which is not? for certainly make themselves wings; they fly away as an eagle toward heaven.

chapters.zh: 不要吃恶眼人的饭，也不要贪他的美味；
chapters.en: Eat thou not the bread of an evil eye, neither desire thou his dainty meats:

chapters.zh: 因为他心怎样思量，他为人就是怎样。他虽对你说，请吃，请喝，他的心却与你相背。
chapters.en: For as he thinketh in his heart, so he: Eat and drink, saith he to thee; but his heart not with thee.

chapters.zh: 你所吃的那点食物必吐出来；你所说的甘美言语也必落空。
chapters.en: The morsel thou hast eaten shalt thou vomit up, and lose thy sweet words.

chapters.zh: 你不要说话给愚昧人听，因他必藐视你智慧的言语。
chapters.en: Speak not in the ears of a fool: for he will despise the wisdom of thy words.

chapters.zh: 不可挪移古时的地界，也不可侵入孤儿的田地；
chapters.en: Remove not the old landmark; and enter not into the fields of the fatherless:

chapters.zh: 因他们的救赎主大有能力，他必向你为他们辨屈。
chapters.en: For their redeemer mighty; he shall plead their cause with thee.

chapters.zh: 你要留心领受训诲，侧耳听从知识的言语。
chapters.en: Apply thine heart unto instruction, and thine ears to the words of knowledge.

chapters.zh: 不可不管教孩童；你用杖打他，他必不至于死。
chapters.en: Withhold not correction from the child: for thou beatest him with the rod, he shall not die.

chapters.zh: 你要用杖打他，就可以救他的灵魂免下阴间。
chapters.en: Thou shalt beat him with the rod, and shalt deliver his soul from hell.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你心若存智慧，我的心也甚欢喜。
chapters.en: My son, if thine heart be wise, my heart shall rejoice, even mine.

chapters.zh: 你的嘴若说正直话，我的心肠也必快乐。
chapters.en: Yea, my reins shall rejoice, when thy lips speak right things.

chapters.zh: 你心中不要嫉妒罪人，只要终日敬畏耶和华；
chapters.en: Let not thine heart envy sinners: but in the fear of the LORD all the day long.

chapters.zh: 因为至终必有善报，你的指望也不致断绝。
chapters.en: For surely there is an end; and thine expectation shall not be cut off.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你当听，当存智慧，好在正道上引导你的心。
chapters.en: Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.

chapters.zh: 好饮酒的，好吃肉的，不要与他们来往；
chapters.en: Be not among winebibbers; among riotous eaters of flesh:

chapters.zh: 因为好酒贪食的，必致贫穷；好睡觉的，必穿破烂衣服。
chapters.en: For the drunkard and the glutton shall come to poverty: and drowsiness shall clothe with rags.

chapters.zh: 你要听从生你的父亲；你母亲老了，也不可藐视他。
chapters.en: Hearken unto thy father that begat thee, and despise not thy mother when she is old.

chapters.zh: 你当买真理；就是智慧、训诲，和聪明也都不可卖。
chapters.en: Buy the truth, and sell not; wisdom, and instruction, and understanding.

chapters.zh: 义人的父亲必大得快乐；人生智慧的儿子，必因他欢喜。
chapters.en: The father of the righteous shall greatly rejoice: and he that begetteth a wise shall have joy of him.

chapters.zh: 你要使父母欢喜，使生你的快乐。
chapters.en: Thy father and thy mother shall be glad, and she that bare thee shall rejoice.

chapters.zh: 我儿，要将你的心归我；你的眼目也要喜悦我的道路。
chapters.en: My son, give me thine heart, and let thine eyes observe my ways.

chapters.zh: 妓女是深坑；外女是窄阱。
chapters.en: For a whore a deep ditch; and a strange woman a narrow pit.

chapters.zh: 他埋伏好像强盗；他使人中多有奸诈的。
chapters.en: She also lieth in wait as a prey, and increaseth the transgressors among men.

chapters.zh: 谁有祸患？谁有忧愁？谁有争斗？谁有哀叹（或译：怨言）？谁无故受伤？谁眼目红赤？
chapters.en: Who hath woe? who hath sorrow? who hath contentions? who hath babbling? who hath wounds without cause? who hath redness of eyes?

chapters.zh: 就是那流连饮酒、常去寻找调和酒的人。
chapters.en: They that tarry long at the wine; they that go to seek mixed wine.

chapters.zh: 酒发红，在杯中闪烁，你不可观看，虽然下咽舒畅，终久是咬你如蛇，刺你如毒蛇。
chapters.en: Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, it moveth itself aright.

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: At the last it biteth like a serpent, and stingeth like an adder.

chapters.zh: 你眼必看见异怪的事（或译：淫妇）；你心必发出乖谬的话。
chapters.en: Thine eyes shall behold strange women, and thine heart shall utter perverse things.

chapters.zh: 你必像躺在海中，或像卧在桅杆上。
chapters.en: Yea, thou shalt be as he that lieth down in the midst of the sea, or as he that lieth upon the top of a mast.

chapters.zh: 你必说：人打我，我却未受伤；人鞭打我，我竟不觉得。我几时清醒，我仍去寻酒。
chapters.en: They have stricken me, I was not sick; they have beaten me, I felt not: when shall I awake? I will seek it yet again.

chapters.zh: 你不要嫉妒恶人，也不要起意与他们相处；
chapters.en: Be not thou envious against evil men, neither desire to be with them.

chapters.zh: 因为，他们的心图谋强暴，他们的口谈论奸恶。
chapters.en: For their heart studieth destruction, and their lips talk of mischief.

chapters.zh: 房屋因智慧建造，又因聪明立稳；
chapters.en: Through wisdom is an house builded; and by understanding it is established:

chapters.zh: 其中因知识充满各样美好宝贵的财物。
chapters.en: And by knowledge shall the chambers be filled with all precious and pleasant riches.

chapters.zh: 智慧人大有能力；有知识的人力上加力。
chapters.en: A wise man strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.

chapters.zh: 你去打仗，要凭智谋；谋士众多，人便得胜。
chapters.en: For by wise counsel thou shalt make thy war: and in multitude of counsellors safety.

chapters.zh: 智慧极高，非愚昧人所能及，所以在城门内不敢开口。
chapters.en: Wisdom too high for a fool: he openeth not his mouth in the gate.

chapters.zh: 设计作恶的，必称为奸人。
chapters.en: He that deviseth to do evil shall be called a mischievous person.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人的思念乃是罪恶；亵慢者为人所憎恶。
chapters.en: The thought of foolishness sin: and the scorner an abomination to men.

chapters.zh: 你在患难之日若胆怯，你的力量就微小。
chapters.en: thou faint in the day of adversity, thy strength small.

chapters.zh: 人被拉到死地，你要解救；人将被杀，你须拦阻。
chapters.en: If thou forbear to deliver drawn unto death, and ready to be slain;

chapters.zh: 你若说：这事我未曾知道，那衡量人心的岂不明白么？保守你命的岂不知道么？他岂不按各人所行的报应各人么？
chapters.en: If thou sayest, Behold, we knew it not; doth not he that pondereth the heart consider ? and he that keepeth thy soul, doth he know ? and shall he render to man according to his works?

chapters.zh: 我儿，你要吃蜜，因为是好的；吃蜂房下滴的蜜便觉甘甜。
chapters.en: My son, eat thou honey, because good; and the honeycomb, sweet to thy taste:

chapters.zh: 你心得了智慧，也必觉得如此。你若找著，至终必有善报；你的指望也不致断绝。
chapters.en: So the knowledge of wisdom unto thy soul: when thou hast found , then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.

chapters.zh: 你这恶人，不要埋伏攻击义人的家；不要毁坏他安居之所。
chapters.en: Lay not wait, O wicked , against the dwelling of the righteous; spoil not his resting place:

chapters.zh: 因为，义人虽七次跌倒，仍必兴起；恶人却被祸患倾倒。
chapters.en: For a just falleth seven times, and riseth up again: but the wicked shall fall into mischief.

chapters.zh: 你仇敌跌倒，你不要欢喜；他倾倒，你心不要快乐；
chapters.en: Rejoice not when thine enemy falleth, and let not thine heart be glad when he stumbleth:

chapters.zh: 恐怕耶和华看见就不喜悦，将怒气从仇敌身上转过来。
chapters.en: Lest the LORD see , and it displease him, and he turn away his wrath from him.

chapters.zh: 不要为作恶的心怀不平，也不要嫉妒恶人；
chapters.en: Fret not thyself because of evil , neither be thou envious at the wicked;

chapters.zh: 因为，恶人终不得善报；恶人的灯也必熄灭。
chapters.en: For there shall be no reward to the evil ; the candle of the wicked shall be put out.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你要敬畏耶和华与君王，不要与反覆无常的人结交，
chapters.en: My son, fear thou the LORD and the king: meddle not with them that are given to change:

chapters.zh: 因为他们的灾难必忽然而起。耶和华与君王所施行的毁灭，谁能知道呢？
chapters.en: For their calamity shall rise suddenly; and who knoweth the ruin of them both?

chapters.zh: 以下也是智慧人的箴言：审判时看人情面是不好的。
chapters.en: These also to the wise. not good to have respect of persons in judgment.

chapters.zh: 对恶人说：你是义人的，这人万民必咒诅，列邦必憎恶。
chapters.en: He that saith unto the wicked, Thou righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:

chapters.zh: 责备恶人的，必得喜悦；美好的福也必临到他。
chapters.en: But to them that rebuke shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them.

chapters.zh: 应对正直的，犹如与人亲嘴。
chapters.en: shall kiss lips that giveth a right answer.

chapters.zh: 你要在外头预备工料，在田间办理整齐，然后建造房屋。
chapters.en: Prepare thy work without, and make it fit for thyself in the field; and afterwards build thine house.

chapters.zh: 不可无故作见证陷害邻舍，也不可用嘴欺骗人。
chapters.en: Be not a witness against thy neighbour without cause; and deceive with thy lips.

chapters.zh: 不可说：人怎样待我，我也怎样待他；我必照他所行的报复他。
chapters.en: Say not, I will do so to him as he hath done to me: I will render to the man according to his work.

chapters.zh: 我经过懒惰人的田地、无知人的葡萄园，
chapters.en: I went by the field of the slothful, and by the vineyard of the man void of understanding;

chapters.zh: 荆棘长满了地皮，刺草遮盖了田面，石墙也坍塌了。
chapters.en: And, lo, it was all grown over with thorns, nettles had covered the face thereof, and the stone wall thereof was broken down.

chapters.zh: 我看见就留心思想；我看著就领了训诲。
chapters.en: Then I saw, considered well: I looked upon received instruction.

chapters.zh: 再睡片时，打盹片时，抱著手躺卧片时，
chapters.en: a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

chapters.zh: 你的贫穷就必如强盗速来，你的缺乏彷佛拿兵器的人来到。
chapters.en: So shall thy poverty come one that travelleth; and thy want as an armed man.

chapters.zh: 以下也是所罗门的箴言，是犹大王希西家的人所誊录的。
chapters.en: These also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

chapters.zh: 将事隐秘乃神的荣耀；将事察清乃君王的荣耀。
chapters.en: the glory of God to conceal a thing: but the honour of kings to search out a matter.

chapters.zh: 天之高，地之厚，君王之心也测不透。
chapters.en: The heaven for height, and the earth for depth, and the heart of kings unsearchable.

chapters.zh: 除去银子的渣滓就有银子出来，银匠能以做器皿。
chapters.en: Take away the dross from the silver, and there shall come forth a vessel for the finer.

chapters.zh: 除去王面前的恶人，国位就靠公义坚立。
chapters.en: Take away the wicked before the king, and his throne shall be established in righteousness.

chapters.zh: 不要在王面前妄自尊大；不要在大人的位上站立。
chapters.en: Put not forth thyself in the presence of the king, and stand not in the place of great :

chapters.zh: 宁可有人说：请你上来，强如在你觐见的王子面前叫你退下。
chapters.en: For better that it be said unto thee, Come up hither; than that thou shouldest be put lower in the presence of the prince whom thine eyes have seen.

chapters.zh: 不要冒失出去与人争竞，免得至终被他羞辱，你就不知道怎样行了。
chapters.en: Go not forth hastily to strive, lest what to do in the end thereof, when thy neighbour hath put thee to shame.

chapters.zh: 你与邻舍争讼，要与他一人辩论，不可泄漏人的密事，
chapters.en: Debate thy cause with thy neighbour ; and discover not a secret to another:

chapters.zh: 恐怕听见的人骂你，你的臭名就难以脱离。
chapters.en: Lest he that heareth put thee to shame, and thine infamy turn not away.

chapters.zh: 一句话说得合宜，就如金苹果在银网子里。
chapters.en: A word fitly spoken apples of gold in pictures of silver.

chapters.zh: 智慧人的劝戒，在顺从的人耳中，好像金耳环和精金的妆饰。
chapters.en: an earring of gold, and an ornament of fine gold, a wise reprover upon an obedient ear.

chapters.zh: 忠信的使者叫差他的人心里舒畅，就如在收割时有冰雪的凉气。
chapters.en: As the cold of snow in the time of harvest, a faithful messenger to them that send him: for he refresheth the soul of his masters.

chapters.zh: 空夸赠送礼物的，好像无雨的风云。
chapters.en: Whoso boasteth himself of a false gift clouds and wind without rain.

chapters.zh: 恒常忍耐可以劝动君王；柔和的舌头能折断骨头。
chapters.en: By long forbearing is a prince persuaded, and a soft tongue breaketh the bone.

chapters.zh: 你得了蜜么？只可吃够而已，恐怕你过饱就呕吐出来。
chapters.en: Hast thou found honey? eat so much as is sufficient for thee, lest thou be filled therewith, and vomit it.

chapters.zh: 你的脚要少进邻舍的家，恐怕他厌烦你，恨恶你。
chapters.en: Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour's house; lest he be weary of thee, and hate thee.

chapters.zh: 作假见证陷害邻舍的，就是大槌，是利刀，是快箭。
chapters.en: A man that beareth false witness against his neighbour a maul, and a sword, and a sharp arrow.

chapters.zh: 患难时倚靠不忠诚的人，好像破坏的牙，错骨缝的脚。
chapters.en: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble a broken tooth, and a foot out of joint.

chapters.zh: 对伤心的人唱歌，就如冷天脱衣服，又如碱上倒醋。
chapters.en: he that taketh away a garment in cold weather, vinegar upon nitre, so he that singeth songs to an heavy heart.

chapters.zh: 你的仇敌若饿了，就给他饭吃；若渴了，就给他水喝；
chapters.en: If thine enemy be hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he be thirsty, give him water to drink:

chapters.zh: 因为，你这样行就是把炭火堆在他的头上；耶和华也必赏赐你。
chapters.en: For thou shalt heap coals of fire upon his head, and the LORD shall reward thee.

chapters.zh: 北风生雨，谗谤人的舌头也生怒容。
chapters.en: The north wind driveth away rain: so an angry countenance a backbiting tongue.

chapters.zh: 宁可住在房顶的角上，不在宽阔的房屋与争吵的妇人同住。
chapters.en: better to dwell in the corner of the housetop, than with a brawling woman and in a wide house.

chapters.zh: 有好消息从远方来，就如拿凉水给口渴的人喝。
chapters.en: cold waters to a thirsty soul, so good news from a far country.

chapters.zh: 义人在恶人面前退缩，好像逿浑之泉，弄浊之井。
chapters.en: A righteous man falling down before the wicked a troubled fountain, and a corrupt spring.

chapters.zh: 吃蜜过多是不好的；考究自己的荣耀也是可厌的。
chapters.en: not good to eat much honey: so to search their own glory glory.

chapters.zh: 人不制伏自己的心，好像毁坏的城邑没有墙垣。
chapters.en: He that no rule over his own spirit a city broken down, without walls.

chapters.zh: 夏天落雪，收割时下雨，都不相宜；愚昧人得尊荣也是如此。
chapters.en: As snow in summer, and as rain in harvest, so honour is not seemly for a fool.

chapters.zh: 麻雀往来，燕子翻飞；这样，无故的咒诅也必不临到。
chapters.en: As the bird by wandering, as the swallow by flying, so the curse causeless shall not come.

chapters.zh: 鞭子是为打马，辔头是为勒驴；刑杖是为打愚昧人的背。
chapters.en: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the ass, and a rod for the fool's back.

chapters.zh: 不要照愚昧人的愚妄话回答他，恐怕你与他一样。
chapters.en: Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest thou also be like unto him.

chapters.zh: 要照愚昧人的愚妄话回答他，免得他自以为有智慧。
chapters.en: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own conceit.

chapters.zh: 藉愚昧人手寄信的，是砍断自己的脚，自受（原文是：喝）损害。
chapters.en: He that sendeth a message by the hand of a fool cutteth off the feet, drinketh damage.

chapters.zh: 瘸子的脚空存无用；箴言在愚昧人的口中也是如此。
chapters.en: The legs of the lame are not equal: so a parable in the mouth of fools.

chapters.zh: 将尊荣给愚昧人的，好像人把石子包在机弦里。
chapters.en: As he that bindeth a stone in a sling, so he that giveth honour to a fool.

chapters.zh: 箴言在愚昧人的口中，好像荆棘刺入醉汉的手。
chapters.en: a thorn goeth up into the hand of a drunkard, so a parable in the mouth of fools.

chapters.zh: 雇愚昧人的，与雇过路人的，就像射伤众人的弓箭手。
chapters.en: The great that formed all both rewardeth the fool, and rewardeth transgressors.

chapters.zh: 愚昧人行愚妄事，行了又行，就如狗转过来吃他所吐的。
chapters.en: As a dog returneth to his vomit, a fool returneth to his folly.

chapters.zh: 你见自以为有智慧的人么？愚昧人比他更有指望。
chapters.en: Seest thou a man wise in his own conceit? more hope of a fool than of him.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人说：道上有猛狮，街上有壮狮。
chapters.en: The slothful saith, a lion in the way; a lion in the streets.

chapters.zh: 门在枢纽转动，懒惰人在床上也是如此。
chapters.en: the door turneth upon his hinges, so the slothful upon his bed.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人放手在盘子里，就是向口撤回也以为劳乏。
chapters.en: The slothful hideth his hand in bosom; it grieveth him to bring it again to his mouth.

chapters.zh: 懒惰人看自己比七个善于应对的人更有智慧。
chapters.en: The sluggard wiser in his own conceit than seven men that can render a reason.

chapters.zh: 过路被事激动，管理不干己的争竞，好像人揪住狗耳。
chapters.en: He that passeth by, meddleth with strife not to him, one that taketh a dog by the ears.

chapters.zh: 人欺凌邻舍，却说：我岂不是戏耍么？他就像疯狂的人抛掷火把、利箭，与杀人的兵器（原文是死亡）。
chapters.en: As a mad who casteth firebrands, arrows, and death,

chapters.zh: a
chapters.en: So the man deceiveth his neighbour, and saith, Am not I in sport?

chapters.zh: 火缺了柴就必熄灭；无人传舌，争竞便止息。
chapters.en: Where no wood is, the fire goeth out: so where no talebearer, the strife ceaseth.

chapters.zh: 好争竞的人煽惑争端，就如余火加炭，火上加柴一样。
chapters.en: coals to burning coals, and wood to fire; so a contentious man to kindle strife.

chapters.zh: 传舌人的言语，如同美食，深入人的心腹。
chapters.en: The words of a talebearer as wounds, and they go down into the innermost parts of the belly.

chapters.zh: 火热的嘴，奸恶的心，好像银渣包的瓦器。
chapters.en: Burning lips and a wicked heart a potsherd covered with silver dross.

chapters.zh: 怨恨人的，用嘴粉饰，心里却藏著诡诈；
chapters.en: He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;

chapters.zh: 他用甜言蜜语，你不可信他，因为他心中有七样可憎恶的。
chapters.en: When he speaketh fair, believe him not: for seven abominations in his heart.

chapters.zh: 他虽用诡诈遮掩自己的怨恨，他的邪恶必在会中显露。
chapters.en: hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the congregation.

chapters.zh: 挖陷坑的，自己必掉在其中；滚石头的，石头必反滚在他身上。
chapters.en: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that rolleth a stone, it will return upon him.

chapters.zh: 虚谎的舌恨他所压伤的人；谄媚的口败坏人的事。
chapters.en: A lying tongue hateth afflicted by it; and a flattering mouth worketh ruin.

chapters.zh: 不要为明日自夸，因为一日要生何事，你尚且不能知道。
chapters.en: Boast not thyself of to morrow; for thou knowest not what a day may bring forth.

chapters.zh: 要别人夸奖你，不可用口自夸；等外人称赞你，不可用嘴自称。
chapters.en: Let another man praise thee, and not thine own mouth; a stranger, and not thine own lips.

chapters.zh: 石头重，沙土沉，愚妄人的恼怒比这两样更重。
chapters.en: A stone heavy, and the sand weighty; but a fool's wrath heavier than them both.

chapters.zh: 忿怒为残忍，怒气为狂澜，惟有嫉妒，谁能敌得住呢？
chapters.en: Wrath cruel, and anger outrageous; but who able to stand before envy?

chapters.zh: 当面的责备强如背地的爱情。
chapters.en: Open rebuke better than secret love.

chapters.zh: 朋友加的伤痕出于忠诚；仇敌连连亲嘴却是多余。
chapters.en: Faithful the wounds of a friend; but the kisses of an enemy deceitful.

chapters.zh: 人吃饱了，厌恶蜂房的蜜；人饥饿了，一切苦物都觉甘甜。
chapters.en: The full soul loatheth an honeycomb; but to the hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.

chapters.zh: 人离本处飘流，好像雀鸟离窝游飞。
chapters.en: As a bird that wandereth from her nest, so a man that wandereth from his place.

chapters.zh: 膏油与香料使人心喜悦；朋友诚实的劝教也是如此甘美。
chapters.en: Ointment and perfume rejoice the heart: so the sweetness of a man's friend by hearty counsel.

chapters.zh: 你的朋友和父亲的朋友，你都不可离弃。你遭难的日子，不要上弟兄的家去；相近的邻舍强如远方的弟兄。
chapters.en: Thine own friend, and thy father's friend, forsake not; neither go into thy brother's house in the day of thy calamity: better a neighbour near than a brother far off.

chapters.zh: 我儿，你要作智慧人，好叫我的心欢喜，使我可以回答那讥诮我的人。
chapters.en: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him that reproacheth me.

chapters.zh: 通达人见祸藏躲；愚蒙人前往受害。
chapters.en: A prudent foreseeth the evil, hideth himself; the simple pass on, are punished.

chapters.zh: 谁为生人作保，就拿谁的衣服；谁为外女作保，谁就承当。
chapters.en: Take his garment that is surety for a stranger, and take a pledge of him for a strange woman.

chapters.zh: 清晨起来，大声给朋友祝福的，就算是咒诅他。
chapters.en: He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.

chapters.zh: 大雨之日连连滴漏，和争吵的妇人一样；
chapters.en: A continual dropping in a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.

chapters.zh: 想拦阻他的，便是拦阻风，也是右手抓油。
chapters.en: Whosoever hideth her hideth the wind, and the ointment of his right hand, bewrayeth .

chapters.zh: 铁磨铁，磨出刃来；朋友相感（原文是磨朋友的脸）也是如此。
chapters.en: Iron sharpeneth iron; so a man sharpeneth the countenance of his friend.

chapters.zh: 看守无花果树的，必吃树上的果子；敬奉主人的，必得尊荣。
chapters.en: Whoso keepeth the fig tree shall eat the fruit thereof: so he that waiteth on his master shall be honoured.

chapters.zh: 水中照脸，彼此相符；人与人，心也相对。
chapters.en: As in water face to face, so the heart of man to man.

chapters.zh: 阴间和灭亡永不满足；人的眼目也是如此。
chapters.en: Hell and destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.

chapters.zh: 鼎为炼银，炉为炼金，人的称赞也试炼人。
chapters.en: the fining pot for silver, and the furnace for gold; so a man to his praise.

chapters.zh: 你虽用杵将愚妄人与打碎的麦子一同捣在臼中，他的愚妄还是离不了他。
chapters.en: Though thou shouldest bray a fool in a mortar among wheat with a pestle, will not his foolishness depart from him.

chapters.zh: 你要详细知道你羊群的景况，留心料理你的牛群；
chapters.en: Be thou diligent to know the state of thy flocks, look well to thy herds.

chapters.zh: 因为资财不能永有，冠冕岂能存到万代？
chapters.en: For riches not for ever: and doth the crown to every generation?

chapters.zh: 干草割去，嫩草发现，山上的菜蔬也被收敛。
chapters.en: The hay appeareth, and the tender grass sheweth itself, and herbs of the mountains are gathered.

chapters.zh: 羊羔之毛是为你作衣服；山羊是为作田地的价值，
chapters.en: The lambs for thy clothing, and the goats the price of the field.

chapters.zh: 并有母山羊奶够你吃，也够你的家眷吃，且够养你的婢女。
chapters.en: And goats' milk enough for thy food, for the food of thy household, and the maintenance for thy maidens.

chapters.zh: 恶人虽无人追赶也逃跑；义人却胆壮像狮子。
chapters.en: The wicked flee when no man pursueth: but the righteous are bold as a lion.

chapters.zh: 邦国因有罪过，君王就多更换；因有聪明知识的人，国必长存。
chapters.en: For the transgression of a land many the princes thereof: but by a man of understanding knowledge the state shall be prolonged.

chapters.zh: 穷人欺压贫民，好像暴雨冲没粮食。
chapters.en: A poor man that oppresseth the poor a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.

chapters.zh: 违弃律法的，夸奖恶人；遵守律法的，却与恶人相争。
chapters.en: They that forsake the law praise the wicked: but such as keep the law contend with them.

chapters.zh: 坏人不明白公义；惟有寻求耶和华的，无不明白。
chapters.en: Evil men understand not judgment: but they that seek the LORD understand all .

chapters.zh: 行为纯正的穷乏人胜过行事乖僻的富足人。
chapters.en: Better the poor that walketh in his uprightness, than perverse ways, though he rich.

chapters.zh: 谨守律法的，是智慧之子；与贪食人作伴的，却羞辱其父。
chapters.en: Whoso keepeth the law a wise son: but he that is a companion of riotous shameth his father.

chapters.zh: 人以厚利加增财物，是给那怜悯穷人者积蓄的。
chapters.en: He that by usury and unjust gain increaseth his substance, he shall gather it for him that will pity the poor.

chapters.zh: 转耳不听律法的，他的祈祷也为可憎。
chapters.en: He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer abomination.

chapters.zh: 诱惑正直人行恶道的，必掉在自己的坑里；惟有完全人必承受福分。
chapters.en: Whoso causeth the righteous to go astray in an evil way, he shall fall himself into his own pit: but the upright shall have good in possession.

chapters.zh: 富足人自以为有智慧，但聪明的贫穷人能将他查透。
chapters.en: The rich man wise in his own conceit; but the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.

chapters.zh: 义人得志，有大荣耀；恶人兴起，人就躲藏。
chapters.en: When righteous do rejoice, great glory: but when the wicked rise, a man is hidden.

chapters.zh: 遮掩自己罪过的，必不亨通；承认离弃罪过的，必蒙怜恤。
chapters.en: He that covereth his sins shall not prosper: but whoso confesseth and forsaketh shall have mercy.

chapters.zh: 常存敬畏的，便为有福；心存刚硬的，必陷在祸患里。
chapters.en: Happy the man that feareth alway: but he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.

chapters.zh: 暴虐的君王辖制贫民，好像吼叫的狮子、觅食的熊。
chapters.en: a roaring lion, and a ranging bear; a wicked ruler over the poor people.

chapters.zh: 无知的君多行暴虐；以贪财为可恨的，必年长日久。
chapters.en: The prince that wanteth understanding also a great oppressor: he that hateth covetousness shall prolong days.

chapters.zh: 背负流人血之罪的，必往坑里奔跑，谁也不可拦阻他。
chapters.en: A man that doeth violence to the blood of person shall flee to the pit; let no man stay him.

chapters.zh: 行动正直的，必蒙拯救；行事弯曲的，立时跌倒。
chapters.en: Whoso walketh uprightly shall be saved: but perverse ways shall fall at once.

chapters.zh: 耕种自己田地的，必得饱食；追随虚浮的，足受穷乏。
chapters.en: He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread: but he that followeth after vain shall have poverty enough.

chapters.zh: 诚实人必多得福；想要急速发财的，不免受罚。
chapters.en: A faithful man shall abound with blessings: but he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be innocent.

chapters.zh: 看人的情面乃为不好；人因一块饼枉法也为不好。
chapters.en: To have respect of persons not good: for for a piece of bread man will transgress.

chapters.zh: 人有恶眼想要急速发财，却不知穷乏必临到他身。
chapters.en: He that hasteth to be rich an evil eye, and considereth not that poverty shall come upon him.

chapters.zh: 责备人的，后来蒙人喜悦，多于那用舌头谄媚人的。
chapters.en: He that rebuketh a man afterwards shall find more favour than he that flattereth with the tongue.

chapters.zh: 偷窃父母的，说：这不是罪，此人就是与强盗同类。
chapters.en: Whoso robbeth his father or his mother, and saith, no transgression; the same the companion of a destroyer.

chapters.zh: 心中贪婪的，挑起争端；倚靠耶和华的，必得丰裕。
chapters.en: He that is of a proud heart stirreth up strife: but he that putteth his trust in the LORD shall be made fat.

chapters.zh: 心中自是的，便是愚昧人；凭智慧行事的，必蒙拯救。
chapters.en: He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.

chapters.zh: 赒济贫穷的，不致缺乏；佯为不见的，必多受咒诅。
chapters.en: He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack: but he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.

chapters.zh: 恶人兴起，人就躲藏；恶人败亡，义人增多。
chapters.en: When the wicked rise, men hide themselves: but when they perish, the righteous increase.

chapters.zh: 人屡次受责罚，仍然硬著颈项；他必顷刻败坏，无法可治。
chapters.en: He, that being often reproved hardeneth neck, shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.

chapters.zh: 义人增多，民就喜乐；恶人掌权，民就叹息。
chapters.en: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice: but when the wicked beareth rule, the people mourn.

chapters.zh: 爱慕智慧的，使父亲喜乐；与妓女结交的，却浪费钱财。
chapters.en: Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father: but he that keepeth company with harlots spendeth substance.

chapters.zh: 王藉公平，使国坚定；索要贿赂，使国倾败。
chapters.en: The king by judgment establisheth the land: but he that receiveth gifts overthroweth it.

chapters.zh: 谄媚邻舍的，就是设网罗绊他的脚。
chapters.en: A man that flattereth his neighbour spreadeth a net for his feet.

chapters.zh: 恶人犯罪，自陷网罗；惟独义人欢呼喜乐。
chapters.en: In the transgression of an evil man a snare: but the righteous doth sing and rejoice.

chapters.zh: 义人知道查明穷人的案；恶人没有聪明，就不得而知。
chapters.en: The righteous considereth the cause of the poor: the wicked regardeth not to know .

chapters.zh: 亵慢人煽惑通城；智慧人止息众怒。
chapters.en: Scornful men bring a city into a snare: but wise turn away wrath.

chapters.zh: 智慧人与愚妄人相争，或怒或笑，总不能使他止息。
chapters.en: a wise man contendeth with a foolish man, whether he rage or laugh, no rest.

chapters.zh: 好流人血的，恨恶完全人，索取正直人的性命。
chapters.en: The bloodthirsty hate the upright: but the just seek his soul.

chapters.zh: 愚妄人怒气全发；智慧人忍气含怒。
chapters.en: A fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise keepeth it in till afterwards.

chapters.zh: 君王若听谎言，他一切臣仆都是奸恶。
chapters.en: If a ruler hearken to lies, all his servants wicked.

chapters.zh: 贫穷人、强暴人在世相遇；他们的眼目都蒙耶和华光照。
chapters.en: The poor and the deceitful man meet together: the LORD lighteneth both their eyes.

chapters.zh: 君王凭诚实判断穷人；他的国位必永远坚立。
chapters.en: The king that faithfully judgeth the poor, his throne shall be established for ever.

chapters.zh: 杖打和责备能加增智慧；放纵的儿子使母亲羞愧。
chapters.en: The rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left bringeth his mother to shame.

chapters.zh: 恶人加多，过犯也加多，义人必看见他们跌倒。
chapters.en: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increaseth: but the righteous shall see their fall.

chapters.zh: 管教你的儿子，他就使你得安息，也必使你心里喜乐。
chapters.en: Correct thy son, and he shall give thee rest; yea, he shall give delight unto thy soul.

chapters.zh: 没有异象（或译：默示），民就放肆；惟遵守律法的，便为有福。
chapters.en: Where no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy he.

chapters.zh: 只用言语，仆人不肯受管教；他虽然明白，也不留意。
chapters.en: A servant will not be corrected by words: for though he understand he will not answer.

chapters.zh: 你见言语急躁的人吗？愚昧人比他更有指望。
chapters.en: Seest thou a man hasty in his words? more hope of a fool than of him.

chapters.zh: 人将仆人从小娇养，这仆人终久必成了他的儿子。
chapters.en: He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become son at the length.

chapters.zh: 好气的人挑启争端；暴怒的人多多犯罪。
chapters.en: An angry man stirreth up strife, and a furious man aboundeth in transgression.

chapters.zh: 人的高傲必使他卑下；心里谦逊的，必得尊荣。
chapters.en: A man's pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.

chapters.zh: 人与盗贼分赃，是恨恶自己的性命；他听见叫人发誓的声音，却不言语。
chapters.en: Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul: he heareth cursing, and bewrayeth not.

chapters.zh: 惧怕人的，陷入网罗；惟有倚靠耶和华的，必得安稳。
chapters.en: The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe.

chapters.zh: 求王恩的人多；定人事乃在耶和华。
chapters.en: Many seek the ruler's favour; but man's judgment from the LORD.

chapters.zh: 为非作歹的，被义人憎嫌；行事正直的，被恶人憎恶。
chapters.en: An unjust man an abomination to the just: and upright in the way abomination to the wicked.

chapters.zh: 雅基的儿子亚古珥的言语就是真言。这人对以铁和乌甲说：
chapters.en: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, the prophecy: the man spake unto Ithiel, even unto Ithiel and Ucal,

chapters.zh: 我比众人更蠢笨，也没有人的聪明。
chapters.en: Surely I more brutish than man, and have not the understanding of a man.

chapters.zh: 我没有学好智慧，也不认识至圣者。
chapters.en: I neither learned wisdom, nor have the knowledge of the holy.

chapters.zh: 谁升天又降下来？谁聚风在掌握中？谁包水在衣服里？谁立定地的四极？他名叫甚么？他儿子名叫甚么？你知道吗？
chapters.en: Who hath ascended up into heaven, or descended? who hath gathered the wind in his fists? who hath bound the waters in a garment? who hath established all the ends of the earth? what his name, and what his son's name, if thou canst tell?

chapters.zh: 神的言语句句都是炼净的；投靠他的，他便作他们的盾牌。
chapters.en: Every word of God pure: he a shield unto them that put their trust in him.

chapters.zh: 他的言语，你不可加添，恐怕他责备你，你就显为说谎言的。
chapters.en: Add thou not unto his words, lest he reprove thee, and thou be found a liar.

chapters.zh: 我求你两件事，在我未死之先，不要不赐给我：
chapters.en: Two have I required of thee; deny me not before I die:

chapters.zh: 求你使虚假和谎言远离我；使我也不贫穷也不富足；赐给我需用的饮食，
chapters.en: Remove far from me vanity and lies: give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with food convenient for me:

chapters.zh: 恐怕我饱足不认你，说：耶和华是谁呢？又恐怕我贫穷就偷窃，以致亵渎我神的名。
chapters.en: Lest I be full, and deny , and say, Who the LORD? or lest I be poor, and steal, and take the name of my God .

chapters.zh: 你不要向主人谗谤仆人，恐怕他咒诅你，你便算为有罪。
chapters.en: Accuse not a servant unto his master, lest he curse thee, and thou be found guilty.

chapters.zh: 有一宗人（宗：原文是代；下同），咒诅父亲，不给母亲祝福。
chapters.en: a generation curseth their father, and doth not bless their mother.

chapters.zh: 有一宗人，自以为清洁，却没有洗去自己的污秽。
chapters.en: a generation pure in their own eyes, and is not washed from their filthiness.

chapters.zh: 有一宗人，眼目何其高傲，眼皮也是高举。
chapters.en: a generation, O how lofty are their eyes! and their eyelids are lifted up.

chapters.zh: 有一宗人，牙如剑，齿如刀，要吞灭地上的困苦人和世间的穷乏人。
chapters.en: a generation, whose teeth swords, and their jaw teeth knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from men.

chapters.zh: 蚂蟥有两个女儿，常说：给呀，给呀！有三样不知足的，连不说够的共有四样：
chapters.en: The horseleach hath two daughters, , Give, give. There are three are never satisfied, , four say not, enough:

chapters.zh: 就是阴间和石胎，浸水不足的地，并火。
chapters.en: The grave; and the barren womb; the earth is not filled with water; and the fire saith not, enough.

chapters.zh: 戏笑父亲、藐视而不听从母亲的，他的眼睛必为谷中的乌鸦啄出来，为鹰雏所吃。
chapters.en: The eye mocketh at father, and despiseth to obey mother, the ravens of the valley shall pick it out, and the young eagles shall eat it.

chapters.zh: 我所测不透的奇妙有三样，连我所不知道的共有四样：
chapters.en: There be three are too wonderful for me, yea, four which I know not:

chapters.zh: 就是鹰在空中飞的道；蛇在磐石上爬的道；船在海中行的道；男与女交合的道。
chapters.en: The way of an eagle in the air; the way of a serpent upon a rock; the way of a ship in the midst of the sea; and the way of a man with a maid.

chapters.zh: 淫妇的道也是这样：他吃了，把嘴一擦就说：我没有行恶。
chapters.en: Such the way of an adulterous woman; she eateth, and wipeth her mouth, and saith, I have done no wickedness.

chapters.zh: 使地震动的有三样，连地担不起的共有四样：
chapters.en: For three the earth is disquieted, and for four it cannot bear:

chapters.zh: 就是仆人作王；愚顽人吃饱；
chapters.en: For a servant when he reigneth; and a fool when he is filled with meat;

chapters.zh: 丑恶的女子出嫁；婢女接续主母。
chapters.en: For an odious when she is married; and an handmaid that is heir to her mistress.

chapters.zh: 地上有四样小物，却甚聪明：
chapters.en: There be four little upon the earth, but they exceeding wise:

chapters.zh: 蚂蚁是无力之类，却在夏天预备粮食。
chapters.en: The ants a people not strong, yet they prepare their meat in the summer;

chapters.zh: 沙番是软弱之类，却在磐石中造房。
chapters.en: The conies a feeble folk, yet make they their houses in the rocks;

chapters.zh: 蝗虫没有君王，却分队而出。
chapters.en: The locusts have no king, yet go they forth all of them by bands;

chapters.zh: 守宫用爪抓墙，却住在王宫。
chapters.en: The spider taketh hold with her hands, and is in kings' palaces.

chapters.zh: 步行威武的有三样，连行走威武的共有四样：
chapters.en: There be three which go well, yea, four are comely in going:

chapters.zh: 就是狮子 ─ 乃百兽中最为猛烈、无所躲避的，
chapters.en: A lion strongest among beasts, and turneth not away for any;

chapters.zh: 猎狗，公山羊，和无人能敌的君王。
chapters.en: A greyhound; an he goat also; and a king, against whom no rising up.

chapters.zh: 你若行事愚顽，自高自傲，或是怀了恶念，就当用手摀口。
chapters.en: If thou hast done foolishly in lifting up thyself, or if thou hast thought evil, thine hand upon thy mouth.

chapters.zh: 摇牛奶必成奶油；扭鼻子必出血。照样，激动怒气必起争端。
chapters.en: Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter, and the wringing of the nose bringeth forth blood: so the forcing of wrath bringeth forth strife.

chapters.zh: 利慕伊勒王的言语，是他母亲教训他的真言。
chapters.en: The words of king Lemuel, the prophecy that his mother taught him.

chapters.zh: 我的儿啊，我腹中生的儿啊，我许愿得的儿啊！我当怎样教训你呢？
chapters.en: What, my son? and what, the son of my womb? and what, the son of my vows?

chapters.zh: 不要将你的精力给妇女；也不要有败坏君王的行为。
chapters.en: Give not thy strength unto women, nor thy ways to that which destroyeth kings.

chapters.zh: 利慕伊勒啊，君王喝酒，君王喝酒不相宜；王子说浓酒在那里也不相宜；
chapters.en: not for kings, O Lemuel, not for kings to drink wine; nor for princes strong drink:

chapters.zh: 恐怕喝了就忘记律例，颠倒一切困苦人的是非。
chapters.en: Lest they drink, and forget the law, and pervert the judgment of any of the afflicted.

chapters.zh: 可以把浓酒给将亡的人喝，把清酒给苦心的人喝，
chapters.en: Give strong drink unto him that is ready to perish, and wine unto those that be of heavy hearts.

chapters.zh: 让他喝了，就忘记他的贫穷，不再记念他的苦楚。
chapters.en: Let him drink, and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.

chapters.zh: 你当为哑巴（或译：不能自辩的）开口，为一切孤独的伸冤。
chapters.en: Open thy mouth for the dumb in the cause of all such as are appointed to destruction.

chapters.zh: 你当开口按公义判断，为困苦和穷乏的辨屈。
chapters.en: Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.

chapters.zh: 才德的妇人谁能得著呢？他的价值远胜过珍珠。
chapters.en: Who can find a virtuous woman? for her price far above rubies.

chapters.zh: 他丈夫心里倚靠他，必不缺少利益 .；
chapters.en: The heart of her husband doth safely trust in her, so that he shall have no need of spoil.

chapters.zh: 他一生使丈夫有益无损。
chapters.en: She will do him good and not evil all the days of her life.

chapters.zh: 他寻找羊羢和麻，甘心用手做工。
chapters.en: She seeketh wool, and flax, and worketh willingly with her hands.

chapters.zh: 他好像商船从远方运粮来，
chapters.en: She is like the merchants' ships; she bringeth her food from afar.

chapters.zh: 未到黎明他就起来，把食物分给家中的人，将当做的工分派婢女。
chapters.en: She riseth also while it is yet night, and giveth meat to her household, and a portion to her maidens.

chapters.zh: 他想得田地就买来；用手所得之利栽种葡萄园。
chapters.en: She considereth a field, and buyeth it: with the fruit of her hands she planteth a vineyard.

chapters.zh: 他以能力束腰，使膀臂有力。
chapters.en: She girdeth her loins with strength, and strengtheneth her arms.

chapters.zh: 他觉得所经营的有利；他的灯终夜不灭
chapters.en: She perceiveth that her merchandise good: her candle goeth not out by night.

chapters.zh: 他手拿撚线竿，手把纺线车。
chapters.en: She layeth her hands to the spindle, and her hands hold the distaff.

chapters.zh: 他张手赒济困苦人，伸手帮补穷乏人。
chapters.en: She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; yea, she reacheth forth her hands to the needy.

chapters.zh: 他不因下雪为家里的人担心，因为全家都穿著朱红衣服。
chapters.en: She is not afraid of the snow for her household: for all her household clothed with scarlet.

chapters.zh: 他为自己制作绣花毯子；他的衣服是细麻和紫色布作的。
chapters.en: She maketh herself coverings of tapestry; her clothing silk and purple.

chapters.zh: 他丈夫在城门口与本地的长老同坐，为众人所认识。
chapters.en: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sitteth among the elders of the land.

chapters.zh: 他做细麻布衣裳出卖，又将腰带卖与商家。
chapters.en: She maketh fine linen, and selleth ; and delivereth girdles unto the merchant.

chapters.zh: 能力和威仪是他的衣服；他想到日后的景况就喜笑。
chapters.en: Strength and honour her clothing; and she shall rejoice in time to come.

chapters.zh: 他开口就发智慧；他舌上有仁慈的法则。
chapters.en: She openeth her mouth with wisdom; and in her tongue the law of kindness.

chapters.zh: 他观察家务，并不吃闲饭。
chapters.en: She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.

chapters.zh: 他的儿女起来，称他有福；他的丈夫也称赞他，
chapters.en: Her children arise up, and call her blessed; her husband , and he praiseth her.

chapters.zh: 说：才德的女子很多，惟独你超过一切。
chapters.en: Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all.

chapters.zh: 艳丽是虚假的，美容是虚浮的；惟敬畏耶和华的妇女必得称赞。
chapters.en: Favour deceitful, and beauty vain: a woman feareth the LORD, she shall be praised.

chapters.zh: 愿他享受操作所得的；愿他的工作在城门口荣耀他。
chapters.en: Give her of the fruit of her hands; and let her own works praise her in the gates.
